# Welcome

Welcome to Uptempo Documentation

Learn how to use Uptempo to plan and measure marketing campaigns, manage budgets and expenses, and more.



# Uptempo Campaign Management

Uptempo Campaign Management

Learn how to use Uptempo Campaign Management to plan, run, and measure your marketing activities.



# Get started with Campaign Management

Uptempo Campaign Management is the system of record for your marketing activities. Use it to plan your marketing campaigns, connect initiatives to funding, and track how your plans are performing.

## Activities and the activity hierarchy

Activities are the basic building block of your marketing plans. You can create different types of activities for various purposes.

Some types of activities represent specific marketing tactics, such as ads, email blasts, or trade show booths. Other types of activities organize your marketing plans into a multi-level structure: for example, you can create an activity to represent a broader campaign, and use that campaign activity to group the various initiatives you plan to execute as part of the campaign.

In Uptempo, these activities and how they are structured into an overall plan are called the activity hierarchy. You manage the activity hierarchy and the activities within it in the Activities section of Uptempo.

Procedure: Open the Activities section

To learn more about how activities and the activity hierarchy work, see [About activities and the activity hierarchy](about-activities.html).

## Activities overview

The Activities section consists of the following areas:

![Activities Overview](images/ActivitiesOverview.png)
1. Activity hierarchy: Your list of marketing activities, organized into a hierarchical parent-child structure (for example into plans, campaigns, tactics, etc.).

2. Toolbar: Use these controls to set which activities are visible, and how they are displayed. The toolbar includes controls for searching, filtering, color-coding, switching between display modes, and more.

3. Activity display: Shows information about the activities in the hierarchy. At any time, you can switch between two different display modes for the activity hierarchy:

1. Timeline (shown): A chronological view of your marketing activities (based on their in-market dates), in which activities are represented as bars on a timeline.

2. Summary: A customizable table view that shows the details of your marketing activities at a glance:
![Summary Display Mode](images/SummaryDisplayMode.png)

4. Activity setup assistant: Click ![Create Activity](images/plus-circle.svg) Create Activity to open the activity setup assistant, which guides you through the process of creating a new activity:
![activity setup assistant Overview](images/ActivitySetupAssistantOverview.png)

5. Activity details panel: Click any existing activity to view detailed information about it in the details panel. You can use an activity's details panel to see its attributes and set their values, connect and manage funding, record performance data, and more:
![details panel Overview](images/DetailsPanelOverview.png)

6. Activity configuration: Settings for administrators to configure Uptempo Campaign Management and the activity hierarchy.

## Navigate the activity hierarchy

Activities in Uptempo Campaign Management are organized into levels according to the hierarchy structure your organization has set up. For example, you might have a structure like this:

Procedure:

To represent this tree-like structure, the activity hierarchy is displayed as a nested list in which the names of all available activities are shown in alphanumeric order (0-9, A-Z).

The activity hierarchy is always visible in the Activities section, under the heading Activity:
![Activity Hierarchy](images/Acti%20Hierach.png)

Note: Note

Depending on your permissions, you may only be able to see part of the full activity hierarchy, or may have restricted access to some activity details or actions.

If you have any questions about your access permissions, contact your organization's Uptempo administrator.

Procedure: Hide or show branches of the activity hierarchy

Procedure: Change the width of the activity hierarchy panel

## Change display modes

The Activities section has two different display modes, which give you different ways of viewing the details of activities in your activity hierarchy:

[Timeline](#timeline)
: Gives you a chronological overview of when your activities are in-market.

[Summary](#summary)
: Lets you see more details about your activities in a table.

### Timeline

The Timeline display mode provides an overview of your activities in a calendar-style timeline. Activities are represented on the timeline as horizontal bars based on their in-market dates, with today's date marked by a vertical green line:

![Activities Overview Timeline](images/ActivitiesOverviewTimeline.png)
Procedure: Switch to Timeline display mode

Procedure: Adjust the timescale

Tip: Tip

If your administrator has configured this feature, can also color-code the activity bars to make it easier to identify activities. For more information, see [Color-code Timeline activity bars](color-coding-timeline-activities.html).

### Summary

The Summary display mode provides an overview of your activities. It consists of a customizable table that displays detailed budget, performance, and other data for each activity. You can use the Summary display mode to compare activities against each other, evaluate activity performance, and compare cost and spend data at a glance.

![Activities Overview Summary](images/ActivitiesOverviewSummary.png)
Procedure: Switch to Summary display mode

The columns in the Summary table are customizable, so you can choose what information you want to see. To learn more about customizing the columns, see [Customize Summary table columns](customize-summary-table.html).

## Customize display settings

You can use the display settings in the toolbar of the Activities section to customize the activity hierarchy to your needs. Use these settings to control which activities are visible in the activity hierarchy, and how those activities are displayed.

Most of the display settings controls in the toolbar are available for both of the display modes (Timeline and Summary). Some toolbar controls are specific to a display mode and are only shown when that display mode is active.

General settings:

The following toolbar controls are available in both the Timeline and Summary display modes:

![Activities Toolbar 1](images/ActivitesToolbar1.png)

1. ![Search](images/search.svg) Search activities field
: Search for activities by name. Type in a keyword to filter the activity hierarchy to display only activities whose name contains the keyword.

2. Select View menu and ![View Options](images/bookmark.svg) View Options
: Create, manage, and select views, which let you save and quickly apply display setting configurations (such as filters, groupings, color coding, etc.).
: * For more information, see [Save activity display settings as a view](save-activity-hierarchy-display-settings-as-views.html)

3. ![Filter Activities](images/filter.svg) Filter Activities
: Filter the activity hierarchy to only display activities that match selected criteria.
: * For more information, see [Filter activities](filter-activities.html).

4. ![Group Activities](images/menu-2.svg) Group Activities
: Group the activities in the hierarchy based on their values for a selected attribute.
: * For more information, see [Group activities](group-activities.html).

5. Timeline / Summary toggle
: Switches the selected display mode between ![Timeline](images/calendar.svg) Timeline and ![Summary](images/spreadsheet.svg) Summary.

6. ![Show Parent Activities](images/git-branch.svg) Show Parent Activities
: Reveals the parent activities of visible activities when filters are applied to the activity hierarchy. This setting is useful for seeing where each visible activity is located within the overall hierarchy, as this context is usually hidden when the activity hierarchy is filtered.
![Activities Toolbar 2](images/ActivitesToolbar2.png)

Tip: Tip

This button is only displayed when filters are applied.

Timeline settings:

The following toolbar controls are only available when the Timeline display mode is used:

![Activities Toolbar 3](images/ActivitesToolbar3.png)

1. ![Select Color-Coding](images/paintbucket-2.svg) Select Color-Coding
: Color-code the activity bars in the timeline based on a selected attribute to make it easier to tell activities apart at a glance. Select an attribute to color the activity bars based on which value the activity has set for that attribute.
: * For more information, see [Color-code Timeline activity bars](color-coding-timeline-activities.html).

2. Select Timeline Scale menu and ![Zoom In](images/zoom-in.svg) Zoom In / ![Zoom Out](images/zoom-out.svg) Zoom Out
: Select the timescale used for the Timeline display mode directly, or zoom in to shorter time scales,

Summary settings:

The following toolbar controls are only available when the Summary display mode is used:

![Activities Toolbar 5](images/ActivitesToolbar5.png)

1. Configure Columns
: Select which columns are displayed in the Summary table, and in what order.
: * For more information, see [Customize Summary table columns](customize-summary-table.html).

Admin-only settings:

The following toolbar controls are only available to administrator users:

![Activities Toolbar 4](images/ActivitesToolbar4.png)

1. ![Settings](images/settings-2.svg) Settings
: Opens the Activity Configuration page, where administrators can configure Uptempo Campaign Management settings.

Tip: Tip

This button is only visible to administrator users.

* For more information, see [Campaign Management configuration overview](cm-config-overview.html).

## Create activities

When you want to add a new activity to the hierarchy, you can do this from either the Timeline or Summary display modes at any time. The activity setup assistant walks you through the process of setting up a new activity step-by-step.

Procedure: Create a new activity

## View an activity's details

For any activity that you have access to, you can view its details (and make changes to the activity) at any time. To do this, open the activity to view its details panel, which is always available in both Timeline or Summary display modes.

Procedure: Open an activity's details panel



# About activities and the activity hierarchy

In Uptempo Campaign Management, an activity is any planned initiative you create to promote a product, service, or message to a defined audience.

Activities are the core building blocks of Campaign Management and are used to represent every level of your marketing hierarchy. An activity can correspond to a high-level initiative (such as a plan, campaign, or program), or to an individual execution, such as a specific tactic. Regardless of its level, each activity is configured, scheduled, tracked, and measured so you can understand its contribution to inquiries, pipeline, and overall marketing impact.

In Uptempo Campaign Management, activities are organized into a hierarchy and displayed in the Activities section:

![Activity Representation](images/Activity%20Representation.png)

For example, a common hierarchy includes levels like:

* Plan

* Campaign

* Program

* Tactic

The structure of your activity hierarchy is defined during implementation in collaboration with your organization's marketing team to ensure it reflects how you plan and manage marketing initiatives. This flexible approach allows Uptempo to support a wide range of marketing models while maintaining consistent planning, measurement, and reporting across all activities.

## Hierarchy paths and relationships

To clarify how hierarchies work in Uptempo, it is helpful to understand the concept of a hierarchy path.

Path
: The direct sequence of connected activities that runs from the highest level of the hierarchy down to a specific activity at a lower level. Each unique route formed by these linkages forms a distinct path through the hierarchy.

In a tree diagram of an activity hierarchy, a path is visualized as a series of lines that connects a sequence of activities vertically through the hierarchy. The following image illustrates multiple paths:

![Path Definition](images/Path%20Definition.png)
In this example, two paths are highlighted. Both start at Plan A and branch to different tactics: one path ends at Tactic LA, the other path ends at Tactic SN. Any valid connection from Plan A to a downstream activity represents a path.

Paths can share portions of the hierarchy. The next image shows three paths that overlap:

![Path Sharing](images/Path%20Sharing.png)
In this case, all three paths pass through Campaign Q and Program FT. This is important, because actions taken on Campaign Q or Program FT affect every path that includes those activities.

Hierarchies also distinguish between ancestors and descendants, based on a defined reference activity (or "starting point"). In the following example, the reference activity is Program FT:

![Ances Desc Def](images/Ances%20Desc%20Def.png)
* All activities upstream from the reference activity are collectively called ancestors. * In this example, Plan A and Campaign Q are ancestors of Program FT.

* All activities downstream from the reference activity are collectively called descendants. * In this example, Tactic SN, Tactic TB, and Tactic WA are descendants of Program FT.

* The immediate ancestor of an activity is referred to as its parent, and any immediate descendant is a child. * In this example, Campaign Q is a parent of Program FT, and Program FT is a child of Campaign Q.

Note: Note

Investments in Uptempo Financial Management are also arranged in a hierarchy. The investments hierarchy works the same way as the activity hierarchy described here, and uses the same terminology to describe relationships and paths.

### Why this matters

Understanding hierarchy paths, ancestors, and descendants is essential when working with activities in Uptempo, because many actions operate across an entire path rather than on a single activity. For example:

* Changes to an activity's attribute data affect all descendant activities, because an activity's attributes are inherited by all descendant activities.

* Rollups for [performance metrics](track-and-analyze-kpis.html) and [estimated costs](track-and-analyze-budgets.html) follow paths through the hierarchy.

* [Rules for connecting activities to investments](rules-for-connecting-investments.html) are based on hierarchy paths and relationships of activities.

By understanding how paths are formed and shared, you can better anticipate the impact of your changes, avoid unintended side effects, and interpret performance data more accurately across your marketing hierarchy.

## Activity types and attributes

In Uptempo, every activity is created with an activity type. The activity type defines what kind of marketing initiative the activity represents, and determines where the activity can be placed in the activity hierarchy.

All activities also have a set of attributes. Attributes are the individual fields that capture information about an activity, such as dates, target audience, product, objective, etc. The specific set of attributes that is attached to an activity is determined by the activity type, so activity types are effectively templates for different kinds of activities. Some attributes are required, while others are optional, depending on how the activity type is configured.

All activity types have the following basic attributes:

* Name: The activity's name, as shown in the activity hierarchy.

* Type: The type of activity, which defines the activity's attributes and where it can be placed in the hierarchy.

* In-Market Dates: The time period during which the activity is expected to be executed in the market. The in-market period of each activity is represented as a horizontal bar in the Timeline display mode.

* Parent: The parent activity, or the activity immediately above the activity in the hierarchy. Valid parent activities are determined by the Type of the activity.

Beyond this basic set of attributes, every activity can also have additional attributes that are defined by its activity type. Each activity also inherits the attributes (and their values) of all of its ancestors in the hierarchy.

## Activity cost estimation

When planning marketing activities, the funding budget may not yet be finalized. If you have an approximate cost in mind, you can use Estimated Costs to record that estimate directly on the activity.

Estimated Costs allow you to capture early cost assumptions in Uptempo so they are available for reference once budgets are finalized. These values are for informational purposes only: they are not included in financial planning in the Budgets or Investments areas, and they are not used in analytics or reporting calculations.

To learn more, see [Track and analyze activity budgets](track-and-analyze-budgets.html).

## Activity funding

An important part of developing your marketing plans is determining how activities will be funded. To understand how an activity fits into your budget, and to measure its ROI, you can use Connect to Spend to connect the activity to one or more investments from the investment hierarchy in Uptempo Financial Management.

After you have connected an activity to funding, you can review planned, expected, committed, and actual spend on the activity throughout its lifecycle. This helps you to see exactly where your marketing dollars are going, and reallocate spend on the fly if you need to.

To learn more, see [Connect investments to track activity funding](connect-investments-to-activities.html).

## Activity performance

To help you measure your marketing plan against the outcomes you are trying to achieve, KPIs and Impact Modeler work together to give you a complete view of how your activities are performing.

### Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)

With KPIs, you can define how success is measured for an activity. You can use them to plan and track operational metrics like engagement and conversion, which provides visibility into execution quality and progress toward planned outcomes.

To learn more, see [Track and analyze KPIs](track-and-analyze-kpis.html).

### Impact Modeling

Impact modeling helps you to demonstrate the impact of your activities on the demand pipeline, and links your marketing efforts to business results.

During the planning phase, you can capture the number of inquiries or leads an activity is expected to generate. These assumptions are fed into a model of your marketing funnel (which includes typical conversion rates between funnel stages, velocity, and average deal sizes). Impact Modeler uses these inputs to project anticipated revenue from an activity, and the timing of that revenue.

By defining the demand an activity is intended to be generated, you can estimate how your initiatives are likely to influence revenue over time. This makes it easier to align marketing plans with organizational goals and to communicate expected value to stakeholders.

Impact modeling also supports better decision-making during planning. By making the expected impact of activities visible upfront, you can prioritize activities with the greatest potential return, and adjust plans before resources are committed. As execution progresses, Impact Modeler provides a reference point for evaluating whether activities are performing as expected, and where corrective action may be needed.

To learn more, see [Track and analyze pipeline impact](track-analyze-pipeline-impact.html).



# Create activities

You can add new activities to the activity hierarchy at any time.

Procedure: Create a new activity

To streamline the activity setup process, you can also create a new activity directly under any existing activity. When you do this:

* The Parent field is automatically set to the appropriate parent activity (the activity under which you created the activity), so you don't need to set it manually.

* The Activity type field is filtered to only show valid options for the selected parent activity, making it easier to choose the correct activity type.

Procedure: Create a new activity as a child of an existing activity



# Edit activities

You can make changes to an existing activity from its details panel.

Procedure: Edit an existing activity

Tip: Tip

You can adjust an activity's in-market dates directly from the Timeline, without needing to open its details panel:

* To adjust the start or end date, drag one end of the activity's bar on the timeline.

* To adjust both the start and end dates together (without changing the duration), drag the activity bar from the middle.



# Move activities

If you need to change the way your activity hierarchy is organized, you can move existing activities to other parts of the hierarchy. For example, you can move an activity from one campaign to another campaign.

You can move an activity under another activity as long as the target activity is a valid parent based on the rules that define your activity hierarchy. Put simply, if the system allows you to create a new activity of that type under a given parent, it also allows you to move an existing activity of that type under that parent.

If you're moving an activity that has descendants in the hierarchy, all descendants will also be moved along with the activity. The existing paths below the moved activity remain the same after the move (to learn more about activity hierarchy relationships and paths, see [About activities and the activity hierarchy](about-activities.html)).

Moving an activity also has the following effects:

* The moved activity (and any descendants) loses attributes inherited from its old ancestor activities, and instead inherits attributes from its new ancestor activities.

* The moved activity's Impact Modeler projections are recalculated.

* Any existing performance data (on the KPIs section) will be permanently deleted from the moved activity.

* If the moved activity is connected to investments, these connections are kept if possible. If the new parent activity is also connected to investments, and these connections are not compatible, the system will ask you if you want to disconnect the moved activity's funding (and adopt the funding of the new parent activity instead), or keep the moved activity's existing funding (and adjust the new parent activity's funding instead).

Procedure: Move an activity to a different part of the hierarchy



# Duplicate activities

You can duplicate an existing activity to create another activity of the same type, and with the same attribute values.

If you duplicate an activity that has descendants in the hierarchy, all descendants (and their types and attribute data) will also be copied. The hierarchy paths of the descendant activities remain the same on the copies as they were on the originals (to learn more about activity hierarchy relationships and paths, see [About activities and the activity hierarchy](about-activities.html)).

Tip: Tip

Because duplicating an activity also copies all descendant activities and preserves their hierarchical path relationships, this function is useful as a quick way to build out branches of your activity hierarchy that have similar structures.

When you duplicate an activity:

* All copies are created under the same parent as the original activity.

* The copies have the same names, relationship structure, and attribute values (as shown on the Details section in the details panel) as the originals.

* Copied activities do not keep the investment connections, estimated costs, connected investments, or performance and impact data of the originals.

Procedure: Duplicate an activity

See [Move activities](move-activities.html) to learn how to move a duplicated activity (and its descendants) to another part of the activity hierarchy.



# Delete activities

If you no longer need an activity, for example because it was created by mistake, you can delete it.

Deleting an activity will remove the activity itself and its data, which includes:

* Attribute data

* Estimated costs

* Connections to investments (and any existing funds allocations)

* Impact and performance data

Warning: Warning

Warning

Deleting an activity permanently removes all data associated with the activity.

Deleted activities or their data can't be recovered.

Procedure: Delete an activity



# Comment on activities

You can use activity comments to discuss activities with other members of your team, and to record updates, changes, and decisions (such as approvals) on activities. Because comments are visible to all users who have access to an activity, they're a great way to provide insight to all stakeholders as you plan, approve, and execute activities.

Activity comments are available on activities of all types. They are displayed in their own Comments panel alongside an activity's details panel. With activity comments, you can:

* Add comments to an activity that are visible to all other users who have view access to that activity

* Organize related comments into conversations with comment threads

* @mention any user to notify them about your comment

* Use emoji in comment text, or as reactions to comments

Procedure: View an activity's comments

Procedure: Add a comment to an activity

Procedure: Edit or delete a comment

Procedure: Manage comment notifications



# Filter activities

By default, the activity hierarchy in Uptempo Campaign Management displays all activities that you have access to, at all levels of the hierarchy. To make a large activity hierarchy easier to work with, you can use filtering to display only activities that match criteria of your choice.

You can filter by the following criteria types:

* Activity type (or type group), for example to only view activities of a specific type

* Attribute value, for example to only view activities that have a specific value for a selected attribute

Note: Notes

* You can specify filters using one or both filter criteria types.

* You can add as many filters as you need. Multiple filters are linked by an AND condition: this means an activity must fulfill the conditions of all filters in order to be displayed.

* Filtering by an attribute will only apply to activities that have the attribute. When you filter by an attribute, you can additionally use the Type filter to ensure that only activities of types that have the selected attribute are displayed.

* If a filter hides an activity, it also hides its child activities.

Procedure: Filter the activity hierarchy

Procedure: Clear all filters from the activity hierarchy



# Group activities

By default, the activity hierarchy in Uptempo Campaign Management displays all activities that you have access to, at all levels of the hierarchy. To make a large activity hierarchy easier to work with, you can use grouping to adjust how they are displayed.

You can group activities together based their selected value for a specified Drop-Down List or Multi-Select List attribute.

In the following example, the selected attribute is Target Persona. Activities are grouped according to their value for this attribute, such as Traveler. Activities to which no value is assigned for the selected attribute are shown grouped under the No value set grouping:

![Grouping no value](images/Grouping%20no%20value.png)

A grouping can only be applied to the activity types to which the attribute is assigned. If you see multiple levels of the activity hierarchy and the hierarchy contains multiple ancestors to whose descendant activities the grouping can be applied, then the descendants are displayed grouped under the respective ancestor.

Note: Note

If you want to see a grouping over all activities to which the attribute is assigned, combine the grouping with a filtering that hides all other activities. An example would be an appropriately restricting type filter.

Procedure: Group activities by attribute

Procedure: Stop grouping activities



# Color-code Timeline activity bars

In the Timeline display mode, marketing activities are represented by horizontal bars. To make it easier to identify activities at a glance, you can color-code the activity bars based on which option of a specified Drop-Down List attribute is set on each activity.

Note: Note

Before an attribute can be used for color-coding, an administrator must configure it by assigning colors to its values. For instructions, see [Configure Timeline activity color-coding](configuring-color-coding-on-the-timeline.html).

When an attribute has been set up for color-coding, it appears as an option in the ![Select Color-Coding](images/paintbucket-2.svg) Select Color-Coding menu in the toolbar on the Timeline.

After you select an attribute from the ![Select Color-Coding](images/paintbucket-2.svg) Select Color-Coding menu, each activity bar is displayed in a predetermined color based on the attribute option set on that activity.

Example
: In this example, the Campaign Objective attribute has color-coding enabled, with the following colors assigned to each attribute value:
: * Sales Enablement: Green

* Reputation: Blue

* Demand Creation: Yellow
: After you select the attribute Campaign Objective in the ![Select Color-Coding](images/paintbucket-2.svg) Select Color-Coding menu, the activity bars for activities with this attribute are displayed in the specified colors according to the attribute value that's assigned to them:
: * The activity bar for the activity "Glow: Part of Your World" is displayed in green, because its Campaign Objective attribute is set to Sales Enablement.

* The activity bar for the activity "Grow and Glow" is displayed in blue, because its Campaign Objective attribute is set to Reputation.

* The activity bar for the activity "Up 'n Glow" is displayed in yellow, because its Campaign Objective attribute is set to Demand Creation.

* The activity bar for the parent activity "2025 Media Plan" is displayed in grey, because it either does not have the Campaign Objective attribute, or does not have a value for that attribute. ![Color-Coding Example](images/ColorCodingExample.png)

Procedure: Color-code activity bars by attribute

Tip: Tip

When you save your display settings as a [view](save-activity-hierarchy-display-settings-as-views.html), your current Color by setting is also saved in the view, and will be set whenever you select the view again.



# Save activity display settings as a view

You can use Views to save your display settings for the Activities section in Uptempo Campaign Management, such as applied [filters](filter-activities.html), [groupings](group-activities.html), and [color-coding](color-coding-timeline-activities.html). Saving display settings as a view lets you quickly and easily apply the same settings again at any time.

You can:

* Save your current display settings in the Timeline or Summary display modes as a view at any time.

* Select a previously created view to quickly apply its saved display settings again.

* Optionally, link views to [column presets](save-summary-table-presets.html) (saved Summary table configurations) to also automatically apply the linked column preset when a view is selected.

## Create views

After you have customized the display settings in the Timeline or Summary display modes, you can save your settings as a view. The following display settings are saved to views:

* ![Filter Activities](images/filter.svg) Filter Activities

* ![Group Activities](images/menu-2.svg) Group Activities

* ![Show Parent Activities](images/git-branch.svg) Show Parent Activities

* ![Select Color-Coding](images/paintbucket-2.svg) Select Color-Coding

* ![Zoom In](images/zoom-in.svg) / ![Zoom Out](images/zoom-out.svg) Select Timeline Scale

You can create as many views as you need. Whenever you select a view later, all the display settings saved to the view are immediately applied again.

Note: Note

By default, views you create are exclusive to you, and aren't available to any other user in your Uptempo instance.

Administrators can additionally create views that are shared with all users in the organization, and manage those views. To learn more, see [Manage activity views for your organization](manage-activity-views-for-your-organization.html).

Procedure: Create a new view

Procedure: Copy a view

## Apply a view

After you have saved display settings as a view, you can quickly recall them by applying the view to the Timeline or Summary display modes in the Activities section.

Procedure: Apply an existing view

## Edit views

After you have created a view, you can make changes to it at any time, such as changing its name and display settings.

If you are an administrator, you can also convert an ![Only for me](images/user.svg) Only for me view into a ![For my organization](images/world.svg) For my organization view (and vice versa).

Procedure: Rename a view

Procedure: Change a view's saved display settings

Procedure: Change a view's type (for administrators)

## Delete views

If you no longer need a view, you can delete it at any time.

Procedure: Delete a view



# Customize Summary table columns

Because the table in the Summary display mode contains a large number of available data columns, you can customize these table columns according to your needs and preferences. You can:

* Select which of the available columns are displayed in the table

* Change the order of the displayed columns

* Pin columns that you want to keep visible as you scroll the table horizontally

Note: Note

Uptempo automatically applies your last saved column configuration the next time you return to the Summary display mode, even if you logged out of Uptempo or closed your browser.

Because your column configuration is saved to your browser's local storage, it will not be applied if you log in to your account with a different browser, and will be reset if you clear your browser data (cache).

## Select columns to display

By default, the table in Summary displays the following columns:

* Activities

* In-market Dates

* Estimated Costs

* Estimated Costs Roll-Up

* Estimated Costs Left

In the Summary table, only the Activities column must always be displayed. For all other available columns, you can add or remove them from the table at any time to display or hide them.

Procedure: Select which columns are displayed

## Reorder and pin columns

You can change the order in which displayed columns are shown in the table, as well as pin columns so that they remain visible when you scroll the table horizontally.

Procedure: Change column display order

Procedure: Pin a column

Tip: Tip

You can save different Summary table column configurations as presets, so that you can easily apply them again. For details, see [Save Summary table columns as presets](save-summary-table-presets.html).



# Save Summary table columns as presets

You can use Column Presets to save your [customized table column settings](customize-summary-table.html) in the Summary display mode. Saving column settings as a column preset lets you quickly and easily apply the same settings again at any time. This makes them a useful shortcut if you have different column configurations that you frequently need to switch between.

You can:

* Save your current customized column configuration in the Summary display mode as a column preset at any time.

* Select a previously created column preset to quickly apply its saved column configuration again.

* Optionally, link column presets to [views](save-activity-hierarchy-display-settings-as-views.html) (saved display settings, like filters or color-coding) to also automatically apply a linked column preset when the view is selected.

## Create column presets

After you [customize the table column configuration](customize-summary-table.html) in the Summary display mode, you can save the configuration as a column preset.

You can create as many column presets as you need. Whenever you select a column preset later, the column configuration saved to the preset is immediately applied again.

Note: Note

By default, column presets you create are exclusive to you, and aren't available to any other user in your Uptempo instance.

Administrators can additionally create column presets that are shared with all users in the organization, and manage those views. To learn more, see [Manage Summary column presets for your organization](manage-summary-column-presets-for-your-organization.html).

Procedure: Create a new column preset

## Copy column presets

You can also create a new column preset by copying an existing column preset.

Procedure: Copy an existing column preset

## Apply column presets

After you have created a column preset, you can quickly recall the saved column configuration in the Summary display mode by applying the preset.

Procedure: Apply an existing column preset

## Edit column presets

After you have created a column preset, you can make changes to it at any time, such as changing its name and saved column configuration.

If you are an administrator, you can also convert an ![Only for me](images/user.svg) Only for me column preset into a ![For my organization](images/world.svg) For my organization column preset (and vice versa).

Procedure: Rename a column preset

Procedure: Change a column preset's saved column configuration

Procedure: Change a column preset's type (for administrators)

## Delete column presets

If you no longer need a column preset, you can delete it at any time.

Procedure: Delete a column preset

## Link column presets to views

If you frequently use a column preset with a particular [view](save-activity-hierarchy-display-settings-as-views.html), you can link the preset to the view. When a column preset is linked to a view, selecting the view also automatically applies the column preset at the same time.

Tip: Tip

In the Select View menu, views that have a linked column preset are marked with the ![Linked column preset](images/columns-vertical.svg) Linked column preset icon:

![Column Presets 6](images/ColumnPresets6.png)

Procedure: Link a column preset to a view

Procedure: Unlink or change the linked column preset on a view



# Connect investments to track activity funding

To represent how the activities in your marketing plan are funded, you can connect investments in Uptempo Financial Management to the marketing activities they are intended to fund.

You can:

* Connect activities at any level of the activity hierarchy to investments (but your administrator may restrict connections to only certain activity types).

* Connect activities to one or multiple investments, at any level of the investment hierarchy (category, sub-category, or line item).

To prevent connections between activities and investments that would cause conflicts or are otherwise not valid, the system automatically enforces a set of rules for making connections. To learn more about these rules, see [Rules for connecting investments](rules-for-connecting-investments.html).

Procedure: Connect an activity to an investment

Procedure: Disconnect an investment from an activity



# Rules for connecting investments

In Uptempo, you can [connect investments to your activities](connect-investments-to-activities.html). However, because both activities and investments are organized into hierarchies, not all activities can be connected to all investments. To prevent funding conflicts and invalid connections, Uptempo automatically applies the following rules whenever activities and investments are connected.

Note: Note

To understand these rules, it's helpful to know how hierarchical relationships and paths work in Uptempo. For details, see [Hierarchy paths and relationships](about-activities.html#hierarchy-paths-and-relationships).

## Rule 1: An activity can only be connected once within a path of the investment hierarchy

There can only be one connection between an activity and any investment within a given path of the investment hierarchy.

In the following example, the Program FT in the activity hierarchy (left) is connected to Sub-Category 25 in the investment hierarchy (right):

![Once In Path](images/Once%20In%20Path.png)
This creates the following connected paths between the activity and investment hierarchies:

![Connected Paths 1](images/Linked%20Paths%201.png)
Although the activity Program FT can have connections to multiple investments, it can't be connected again anywhere within the path of the investment hierarchy that Sub-Category 25 is in. For example, it can't be connected to Line Item 21, or Category 2, as they are within the same path as Sub-Category 25, which it is already connected to.

However, Program FT can be connected to multiple investments as long as they are in different paths of the investment hierarchy, as shown here:

![Connected Paths 2](images/Linked%20Paths%202.png)

## Rule 2: Connections must follow ancestor-to-ancestor and descendant-to-descendant relationships

When a path in the activity hierarchy is connected to a path in the investment hierarchy, additional connections must respect the relative positions of activities and investments in their respective hierarchies.

In the following example, Program FT in the activity hierarchy (left) is connected to both Sub-Category 25 and Sub-Category 43 in the investment hierarchy (right):

![Ancest Desc Path](images/Ancest%20Desc%20Path.png)
This creates the following connected paths between the activity and investment hierarchies:

![Rule 3 Paths](images/Rule%203%20Paths.png)
Because of these existing connections, Campaign Q (an ancestor of Program FT) can only be connected only to ancestors of Sub-Category 25 and Sub-Category 43.

For example, Campaign Q can be connected to Category 2:

![Ancest Desc Path 4](images/Ancest%20Desc%20Path%204.png)
But connecting Campaign Q to descendants of Sub-Category 25 or Sub-Category 43 (such as Sub-Category 31 or Line Item 50) is not allowed.

Similarly, descendants must connect only to descendants. As a result, Tactic TB (a descendant of Program FT) can be connected only to descendants of Sub-Category 25 and Sub-Category 43. Valid connection targets include:

* Sub-Category 31

* Line Item 21

* Line Item 47

* Line Item 50

One valid configuration is shown in the following example:

![Ancest Desc Path 3](images/Ancest%20Desc%20Path%203.png)

Connected paths restrict further connections
: After the initial connection between an activity hierarchy path and an investment hierarchy path is made, all additional connections must stay within those paths. Connections outside the established paths are not allowed.
: In the following examples, the red lines represent connections that are not allowed because the initial connections for Program FT defined the connected paths within the investment plan:
: * Campaign Q can't be connected to another path within the Budget 5 plan: ![Not Allowed 1](images/Not%20Poss%201.png) Note: Note However, Campaign Q can be connected to a different investment plan: see [Rule 3](#rule-3-the-highest-connected-activity-can-be-connected-to-a-different-investment-plan).

* Tactic WA can't be connected to Line Item 61: ![Not Allowed 2](images/Not%20Poss%202.png)

* Program AZ (a sibling of Program FT) can't be connected to Sub-Category 31: ![Not Allowed 3](images/Not%20Poss%203.png)
: As a result, the initial connections between activities and investments are critical: they determine which paths are available for all subsequent connections.

## Rule 3: The highest connected activity can be connected to a different investment plan

Within a single investment plan, the first connection determines the available paths for subsequent connections, as described in [Rule 2](#rule-2-connections-must-follow-ancestor-to-ancestor-and-descendant-to-descendant-relationships). However, you can also make connections to additional investment plans.

Only the highest connected activity in the activity hierarchy can be connected to another investment plan.

In this example, Campaign Q is the highest connected activity and can therefore be connected to a different investment plan:

![Ancest Desc Path 4](images/Ancest%20Desc%20Path%204.png)
In this example, there are no connections above Program FT, so it is the highest connected activity and can be connected to another investment plan:

![Ancest Desc Path 3](images/Ancest%20Desc%20Path%203.png)



# Track and analyze activity budgets

In Uptempo Campaign Management, Estimated Costs and Connect to Spend help you to plan, track, and evaluate your marketing budgets more effectively.

With Estimated Costs, you can define the expected cost of activities during the planning phase, giving you a forward-looking view of how your budget is allocated across campaigns, activities, and time periods. This helps you to forecast more accurately, identify budget constraints early, and align planned spend with your marketing objectives.

Connect to Spend links your activities to spend data from Uptempo Financial Management. Making spend data available on your activities gives you clearer insight into budget performance, which lets you easily monitor how spend is performing and reallocate budget if needed.

## How activity budget tracking works

When you are planning your marketing activities, the budgets that will fund them may not yet be finalized. If you have an initial sense of what an activity is likely to cost, you can use Estimated Costs to record that estimate directly on the activity.

You can enter estimated costs at any level of your activity hierarchy. All estimated costs automatically aggregate up to the top of the activity hierarchy along [hierarchy paths](about-activities.html#hierarchy-paths-and-relationships), so you can plan costs top-down or bottom-up, and get insight into anticipated costs at all levels.

Note: Note

The estimated cost amounts you enter are intended for activity planning and reference purposes only. They are not used by Uptempo Financial Management, and they are not included in Uptempo Analytics reports.

After budgets are set, you can [use Connect to Spend to connect investments to activities](connect-investments-to-activities.html). When you do this, budget data from the connected investment is displayed directly on the activity, so you can easily view the activity's planned, expected, committed, and actual spend, and compare it to the cost assumptions you made during the planning phase.

## Track estimated costs on activities

For each activity, you can enter what you expect the activity to cost during the planning phase. Estimated costs automatically roll up to ancestor activities, so you can both record and view estimated costs at any level of the hierarchy.

After you enter the estimated cost for an activity, you can reference it again at any time.

Tip: Tip

Estimated costs work with both top-down and bottom-up planning:

* Top-down planning: Enter estimated costs for higher-level activities early in the planning process. Later, distribute the amounts you entered earlier to lower levels of the hierarchy by entering estimated costs on specific activities as you create them.

* Bottom-up planning: Start by entering estimated costs for activities at the lowest level of the hierarchy. Later, view parent activities to see roll-up amounts from all child activities, and optionally override it with a value set on the parent activity itself.

Procedure: Add a cost estimate to an activity

Procedure: View an activity's estimated costs

## Track spend on activities

After an activity has been [connected to an investment](connect-investments-to-activities.html), you can view associated spend data from Uptempo Financial Management in the Budget section of the activity's details panel at any time.

Procedure: View an activity's spend data

### Break down activity spend data by time period

Note: Note

This feature is available in Preview with the [2026.3 release](release-2026-3.html). To enable this feature in your Uptempo instance, please contact your Customer Success Manager, or Uptempo Support.

By default, the system displays total amounts for the spend data on activities, representing the full time period covered by the connected investment.

Because timing is often a critical consideration with budget spend, you can also view more granular annual and quarterly breakdowns of activity spend data. This gives you a more detailed view of exactly when spend is happening, which can help you to spot potential issues (such as overspending in a particular quarter).

* Single year: On activities connected to investments from one budget year, select the Quarterly option to see spend data broken down by quarter: ![Breakdown single year quarterly](images/BreakdownSingleYearQuarterly.png)

* Multiple years: On activities connected to investments from more than one budget year, a Yearly option is also available (in addition to Quarterly). With both options, you can use the FY year tabs to choose the fiscal year for which you want to view data: ![Breakdown multi year quarterly](images/BreakdownMultiYearQuarterly.png)

Note: Note

Quarterly and yearly spend data breakdowns are only shown if a date range has been set on the corresponding budget columns in Uptempo Financial Management.

You can view spend data breakdowns by time period at any level of the activity hierarchy. On higher-level activities, the system lists amounts aggregated from connected investments on lower-level activities (Roll-up) separately from amounts associated with investments connected to the activity itself (Connected):

![Breakdown roll upvs connected](images/BreakdownRollUpvsConnected.png)

### Understanding spend data panel amounts

Both activities and investments exist within multi-level hierarchies, and you can make connections between activities and investments at multiple levels. The amounts shown when you expand the spend data panels are designed to make it easy to understand how connections made at lower levels of the activity hierarchy impact funding at the level you're currently viewing.

For example, assume you have an activity called Demand Gen Campaign, and you connect it to a corresponding budget category in Uptempo Financial Management called Demand Gen Campaign Budget:

* The Demand Gen Campaign Budget budget category contains line items for specific funding amounts that total $15,000. Therefore, when you connect Demand Gen Campaign to this budget category, you know that you have $15,000 available to fund the campaign.

* As you plan out the campaign, you create child activities under Demand Gen Campaign for each of the tactics you plan to use. You connect each child activity to a corresponding line item in the connected Demand Gen Campaign Budget budget category to track how much of the overall budget each tactic is consuming. * For example, you allocate $7,000 to Tactic A and $3,000 to Tactic B. Together, these two tactics consume $10,000 of the $15,000 available.

At this point, the Details > Budget section of the Demand Gen Campaign activity looks like this:
![Activity Rollup Example](images/ActivityRoll-UpExample.png)

Here, you can see at a glance that:

* The total planned spend for this campaign is $15,000.

* The portion of the investment that is already consumed by child activities is $10,000 (the amount shown on the Activity Roll-Up row).

* The amount of the total planned spend that remains available for activities in this campaign is $5,000 (the amount shown on the Demand Gen Campaign Budget row). * This amount is calculated by subtracting the $10,000 Activity Roll-Up amount from the $15,000 Planned Spend total amount.

This breakdown allows you to quickly understand exactly how much has been spent, and how much remains available to fund other tactics (or reassign elsewhere), when looking at any level of your activity hierarchy.



# Track and analyze pipeline impact

In Uptempo Campaign Management, impact modeling helps you to demonstrate the impact of your activities on the demand pipeline, and links your marketing efforts to business results.

On your Uptempo activities, you can record the demand you expect the activity to generate. The system uses the planned demand numbers you enter to calculate projections of how your initiatives are likely to influence revenue over time. This makes it easier to align marketing plans with organizational goals, and to communicate expected value to stakeholders.

Impact modeling also supports better decision-making during planning. By making the expected impact of activities visible upfront, you can prioritize activities with the greatest potential return, and adjust plans before resources are committed. As execution progresses, the impact model provides a reference point for evaluating whether activities are performing as expected, and where corrective action may be needed.

## How impact modeling works

During the planning phase, you can enter the estimated number of generated inquiries or leads that you're targeting for an activity. Your estimated demand numbers are then fed into a detailed model of your demand pipeline, which uses them to project anticipated revenue from each activity, and when that revenue is expected to be realized.

During the execution phase, you can record actual results for inquiries or leads created by the same activities, as well as for revenue generated by closed deals. By comparing targets and results in real time, you can quickly see what’s working and what’s not, so that you can make informed decisions about how to adjust your strategy.

You can enter impact data for planned targets and actual results at any level of your activity hierarchy. All impact data automatically aggregates up to the top of the activity hierarchy along [hierarchy paths](about-activities.html#hierarchy-paths-and-relationships), so you can get full insight into performance at every level of your marketing plan.

### About the pipeline impact model

Your Uptempo administrator sets up the pipeline impact model that the system uses to generate projections. The impact model consists of the following parts:

* The structure of your organization's demand generation funnel. This consists of the stages that you track as customers move through the sales pipeline, from lead to closed/won deal.

* Your baseline funnel performance metrics. This includes typical conversion rates (the rate at which leads move from one funnel stage to the next), velocities (how fast they move between stages), and the average value of a closed/won deal.

* Segment-specific custom metrics to enhance the baseline metrics. These provide alternative, more specific funnel performance assumptions based on a combination of dimensions, such as activity attribute values and activity groups. For example, you can set overrides for scenarios such as if the average deal size for a certain product is higher or lower, or if deals in a particular market close faster (or slower) than average.

When you enter a planned demand target for an activity, Uptempo feeds this input into the pipeline impact model and generates a projection of how much revenue the activity is expected to generate, and when. To do this, it uses segment-specific metrics if available (determined by the attributes set on the activity), or otherwise uses the baseline performance metrics if there is no applicable segment.

## View activity impact data

For each activity, the Impact section of the activity's details panel contains its impact modeling data. Here, you can view existing impact data and projections, as well as enter planned and actual impact results.

Procedure: View an activity's impact data

## Planned demand targets and projected impact

You enter the target demand that you plan to generate in the Planned tab of an activity's Impact section. After you enter a target, the impact model displays a projection of how the planned demand is expected to influence revenue over the time period during which the activity is in-market.

Note: Note

You can enter targets at any level of the activity hierarchy. However, for any given [hierarchy path](about-activities.html#hierarchy-paths-and-relationships), a KPI value can be entered and maintained at only one level. After a value is set at one level, it cannot be entered or edited at other levels along the same path.

Activities at higher levels automatically display roll-up impact data aggregated from all lower levels and use blended model assumptions from those levels when generating projections. Lower-level (descendant) activities, by contrast, do not display data entered at higher levels or projections derived from that data.

For this reason, we recommend entering demand targets at the lowest level of the activity hierarchy. Doing so ensures that impact data and projections are visible and consistent at every level above.

Procedure: Enter planned demand targets for an activity

### Adjust how planned demand is distributed

By default, the impact model projection assumes that the planned demand is generated evenly across the period defined by the activity's in-market dates. For activities with longer in-market periods, the generated demand might be unevenly distributed within the in-market period. For example, you might see a peak in demand during the holiday period followed by a dip afterward, with a corresponding peak and dip in revenue.

To generate more accurate revenue projections for these situations, you can adjust how the planned demand is distributed across the in-market period.

Procedure: Adjust planned demand distribution for an activity

### View impact projections

After you enter a planned demand target for an activity, the system uses it to generate and display an impact projection chart in the activity's details panel on the Impact > Planned tab:

![Impact tab 2](images/Impact%20tab%202.png)
* After you enter planned demand targets, the chart displays line graphs to visualize planned demand (in purple) and planned revenue (in yellow) over time.

* After you add actual results, the chart also displays line graphs to visualize actual demand (in orange), and actual revenue (in green), for comparison.

* The time period represented by the chart's x-axis starts with the activity's in-market start date. It ends on the date the revenue from the last closed/won deal is planned to be realized, the date when the last actual revenue was generated, or the current date (whichever is latest).

Note: Where's the chart?

To generate an impact projection chart, the system requires:

* For planned demand and revenue: Planned impact data (on the Impact > Planned tab) and in-market dates (in the Details section).

* For actual demand and revenue: Actual impact data (on the Impact > Actual tab).

If you've entered only planned impact data but are not seeing a chart, check that you've also specified in-market dates for the activity (the system needs these dates to plot planned demand and revenue over time).

Tip: Tip

Place the pointer on the chart to see the exact value on any line graph at the pointer's position.

When the system has generated an impact projection, you can also see details about the funnel assumptions that the impact model used to generate the projections.

Procedure: View funnel assumptions for an activity's projections

## Actual demand and revenue results

You enter the actual demand and revenue results achieved in the Actual tab of an activity's Impact section. After you enter actual results for a KPI, Uptempo automatically displays them on the impact projection chart (on the Planned tab), so you can visualize how the activity performed compared to the projections:

![Planned Sub Tab](images/PlannedSubTab.png)
Procedure: Enter actual demand and revenue results for an activity



# Track and analyze KPIs

In Uptempo Campaign Management, Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) help you to define how success is measured for the activities in your marketing plan beyond the [demand generation funnel](track-analyze-pipeline-impact.html). Use KPIs to plan and track operational metrics like engagement and conversion, and gain visibility into execution quality and progress toward planned outcomes.

You can use KPIs to:

* Evaluate performance: Compare actual impressions against planned values to quickly assess if your campaign is meeting its objectives.

* Inform resource allocation: Identify which activities are delivering the best results and adjust your budget accordingly.

* Analyze trends: Track monthly performance patterns for your campaign, noting which months performed best to inform future planning.

* Report on performance: Use the clear visual indicators to quickly build executive reports showing campaign success rates across your marketing portfolio.

## How KPIs work

KPIs are specific to activity types. For each activity, you set targets for the KPIs assigned to activities of that type during the planning phase. During the execution phase, you can enter actual KPI results for the same activities. By comparing targets and results in real time, you can quickly see what’s working and what’s not, so that you can make informed decisions about how to adjust your strategy.

You can enter KPI data for planned targets and actual results at any level of your activity hierarchy. All KPI data automatically aggregates up to the top of the activity hierarchy along [hierarchy paths](about-activities.html#hierarchy-paths-and-relationships), so you can get full insight into performance at every level of your marketing plan.

## View activity KPI data

For each activity, you can see the KPIs assigned to activities of that type, in the KPIs section of the activity's details panel. Here, you can view existing KPI data, as well as enter KPI target and result data.

Procedure: View an activity's KPIs

## Planned KPI targets

You enter the target results that you plan to achieve for each KPI in the Planned tab of an activity's KPIs section.

KPIs shown in the Planned tab are organized into sections. Depending on the activity's position in the hierarchy, up to three sections are shown:

Higher Levels
: This section displays KPI target data set on activities that are above the current activity in the hierarchy (ancestors). This section is only shown if there is KPI target data at higher levels. For KPIs to be displayed here, the KPIs must also be assigned to type of activity you're currently viewing.

Active Level
: This section displays KPI target data for the activity you're currently viewing, and is always visible. You enter KPI data for the current activity in this section.

Lower Levels
: This section displays aggregated KPI target data set on activities that are below the current activity in the hierarchy (descendants). This section is only shown if there is KPI target data at lower levels. For KPIs to be displayed here, the KPIs must also be assigned to type of activity you're currently viewing.

Procedure: Enter planned KPI targets for an activity

## Actual KPI results

You enter the actual results achieved for each KPI in the Actual tab of an activity's KPIs section. After you enter actual results for a KPI, Uptempo automatically displays a comparison to the KPI's planned target, so you can see at a glance how the activity performed.

Procedure: Enter and compare actual KPI results for an activity

When you enter actual results for a KPI, you can choose whether you want to enter and display actual results as a single period for a selected date range, or broken down by quarter or month/period.

Procedure: Change time granularity and date range for actual results



# Campaign Management configuration overview

Learn how you can configure Uptempo Campaign Management to reflect the way your organization defines, plans, and measures marketing activities.

As an administrator, you can:

* Set up the different types of marketing activities that your organization uses.

* Create a hierarchical structure to organize your activities, such as into plans and campaigns.

* Define the kinds of data you want to record in relation to your marketing activities to support effective planning and reporting.

* Configure how you want to measure the effectiveness of your marketing activities, such as their impact on your funnel, or which KPIs you track.

* Customize Uptempo Campaign Management to suit your organization's needs and preferences.

## The configuration process

Tip: Tip

An Uptempo specialist likely configured Campaign Management for you during the implementation phase.

If you have an existing configuration and need to make changes to it, use the navigation menu to jump straight to the relevant pages in this section.

If you're setting up Campaign Management in a new Uptempo instance, we recommend the following order of operations:

### Step 1: Create attributes

Begin by creating attributes to record the types of data you want to track on your marketing activities.

An attribute is a structured piece of data that describes some aspect of a marketing activity. You use attributes to capture key details about the activities in your marketing plans in a consistent way, such as:

* Objectives

* Status

* Geographic region or market

* Target segment or persona

* Marketing channel

After you create an attribute, you can use it on your activities to ensure that the attribute's data is captured whenever activities are created.

Learn more
: To learn about attributes and how to create and manage them, see [Activity attribute configuration overview](activity-attribute-config-overview.html).

### Step 2: Build the activity hierarchy

The next step is to set up your activity hierarchy to define the kinds of marketing activities you plan and track, and how they are organized.

The activity hierarchy structures your activities multiple levels, where broader "parent" activities sit above more detailed and specific "child" activities. This structure reflects the way most marketing teams organize their work: by building high-level plans that are divided into larger initiatives, which in turn are made up of low-level actions that contribute to the .

To build your activity hierarchy, you:

1. First, create activity type groups to organize similar and related activity types for easier management

2. Then, create activity types, predefined templates to standardize the way different kinds of marketing activities are planned and tracked

3. Finally, create activity rules to define the parent-child relationships between activity types in your hierarchy

Learn more
: * To learn more about the activity hierarchy see [Activity hierarchy configuration overview](activity-hierarchy-config-overview.html).

* To learn how to create activity type groups, see [Create activity type groups](create-activity-type-groups.html).

* To learn how to create activity types, see [Create and configure activity types](create-and-configure-activity-types.html).

* To learn how to create activity rules, see [Create activity rules](create-activity-rules.html).

### Step 3: Set up activity access controls

With your activity hierarchy in place, it's important to determine who in your Uptempo environment can access your marketing activities and their associated data. To do that, you set up activity access controls.

Activity access controls are used to define exactly who (users) can access what (activities and their data) and how (the permitted actions). Setting up access controls helps to protect sensitive or privileged information, and prevents unauthorized changes to ensure the integrity of your marketing data.

Learn more
: To learn more about how activity access controls work, and how to set them up, see [Activity access control configuration overview](activity-access-control-config-overview.html).

### Step 4: Configure measurement tools

After you have defined how your marketing activities should be structured, the next step is determining how you want to measure their performance.

There are two ways to measure your marketing activities in Campaign Management: Impact Modeler, and Key Performance Indicators.

* Impact Modeler lets you model the effect of your activities on your demand funnel. For each activity, you can enter assumptions for factors like conversion rate and velocity between stages and the expected deal size, and Impact Modeler generates revenue projections to show how your marketing activities are driving revenue.

* Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) are essentially a special type of attribute that you can add to activity types to record performance metrics of your choice. On activities, you can enter both planned and actual KPI results, and track and compare them over time.

Learn more
: * To learn how to set up and use Impact Modeler, see [Configure the Impact Modeler pipeline model](configure-impact-modeler.html).

* To learn how to set up and manage KPIs, see [Configure Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)](creating-and-managing-key-performance-indicators-kpis.html).

### Step 5: Customize preferences and other settings

Finally, configure the various settings to customize the behavior and appearance of Uptempo Campaign Management according to your organization's needs. You can configure settings like your primary planning currency, predefined activity views, color-coding, and more.

Learn more
: To learn how to configure preferences for your Uptempo Campaign Management instance, see [Activity hierarchy configuration overview](activity-hierarchy-config-overview.html).



# Activity attribute configuration overview

As an administrator, you create attributes and attach them to activity type groups and activity types. When users create or edit activities in Campaign Management, those activities display the attached attributes (according to their activity type) for data entry.

## Attribute types

When you create an attribute, you start by selecting the attribute's type. The type determines what kind of data value the attribute field will accept and store (such as numbers, text, dates, etc.).

Each attribute type is also associated with a specific user interface (UI) element. Users use this UI element to set a value for the attribute when they create or edit an activity.

Note: Note

You set an attribute's type when you create the attribute. The attribute type can't be changed later.

Uptempo Campaign Management supports a variety of different attribute types for different kinds of data:

| Type |Description |Example |
------------------------------
| Date |Calendar-style selector for date values. |Start or End Date |
| Drop-Down List |Predefined list of options where a single option can be selected. |Regions |
| Multi-Select List |Predefined list of options where multiple options can be selected. Optionally, attribution percentages can be assigned to each selected value. |Marketing Objectives |
| Number or Metric |Single-line text field for (non-currency) numeric values. |Expected Impressions |
| Rich Text |Large text field for formatted text, with a WYSIWYG editor. |Plan on a Page |
| Text or Reference # |Single-line text field that accepts letters, numbers, and symbols. |Description |

### Date

Data Type: Single date values in the format MM/DD/YYYY.

UI Element: Date picker that displays a calendar. Users can click on a date in the calendar to set the date value, or enter it manually into the input field.

![Attribute Type Example Date](images/AttributeTypeExampleDate.png)

### Drop-Down List

Data Type: Predefined options.

UI Element: Drop-down type menu that displays a list of predefined options. Users can click on a single option in the list to select it. Users can also type a keyword into the field to filter the options list.

![Attribute Type Example Dropdown](images/AttributeTypeExampleDropdown.png)

### Multi-Select List

Data Type: Predefined options, optionally with user-customizable percentages.

UI Element: Drop-down type menu that displays a list of predefined options. Users can click on one or multiple options in the list to select them. Users can also type a keyword into the field to filter the options list.

![Attribute Type Example Multi Select](images/AttributeTypeExampleMultiSelect.png)
Optionally, users can also enter a percentage value for each selected option, for example to indicate each option's share:

![Attribute Type Example Multi Select Percentage](images/AttributeTypeExampleMultiSelectPercentage.png)

### Number or Metric

Data Type: Numeric values.

UI Element: Input field that accepts values containing only numeric characters (0-9). Values that include non-numeric characters are rejected.

![Attribute Type Example Number](images/AttributeTypeExampleNumber.png)

### Rich Text

Data Type: Formatted rich text (including emoji and other special character sets).

UI Element: Large text input field with a visual formatting control bar (WYSIWYG editor). Supported formatting options:

#### Text styles

* Normal (paragraph)

* Headings (h1 - h3)

* Hyperlink (clickable)

* Blockquote

#### Text formatting

* Bold

* Italic

* Underline

#### List styles

* Bullet

* Numbered

![Rich Text Attribute](images/RichTextAttribute.png)
Note: Note

The following limitations apply specifically to Rich Text attributes:

* When you view the details panel of an activity whose parent activity has one or more Rich Text attributes, these attributes are not included in the Show inherited attributes section which displays attributes (and their values) inherited from the parent activity.

* Rich Text attributes are not included in any Uptempo reporting functionality.

* Rich Text attributes are not support for filtering and sorting activities, or for Outbound Actions.

### Text or Reference#

Data Type: Alphanumeric values.

UI Element: Input field that accepts values containing any combination of letter characters, number characters, and special characters.

![Attribute Type Example Text](images/AttributeTypeExampleText.png)
Note: Note

The Text or Reference # attribute type supports characters from the Unicode Basic Multilingual Plane. This includes characters for all modern languages, such as diacritics (such as umlauts, accents, etc.), logographs (such as characters for Chinese, Japanese, Korean, etc.), and various other symbols.

## Attribute behavior

### Inheritance

In the [activity hierarchy](about-activities.html), activities inherit all attributes of all ancestor activities, along with their attribute values. To avoid inconsistencies due to multiple defined values for an attribute, an attribute can only be used once in a branch of the activity hierarchy. This means that you can't create an [activity rule](create-activity-rules.html) where a child activity has the same attribute as a parent activity.

### Dependencies

If two Drop-Down List or Multi-Select List attributes are logically related, you can create an attribute dependency to link them. In an attribute dependency, the value selected for the first attribute (the controlling attribute) determines which values can be selected in the second attribute (the dependent attribute).

For more information, see [Set up attribute dependencies](attribute-dependencies.html).



# Create and configure attributes

You can create a new attribute to record data on your activity types at any time.

Procedure: Create a new attribute

Tip: Tip

You can also add an attribute to an [activity type group](create-activity-type-groups.html). This will add it to all activity types in the group.



# Configure attribute list options

Attributes with the types Drop-Down List and Multi-Select List have additional settings. These settings configure the predefined set of options that a user can select from the list.

When you configure list options for these attribute types, you can:

* Add list options, rename them and change their display order

* Manage list options in bulk with the export and import features

* Deactivate list options

* Delete list options (and replace them with other list options on activities where they are in use)

## Add and manage list options

Procedure: Add and configure list options individually

Procedure: Add list options in bulk

## Enable the Show Percentages option (Multi-Select List)

On Multi-Select List attributes, you can enable an additional Show Percentages option. When this option is enabled, users can specify a percentage value for each of the options that they select:

![MultiSelectPercentage](images/MultiSelectPercentage.png)

Examples
: You can use the Show Percentages option for attributes where you want to record the relative weighting or contribution of each selection.
: Here are some example use cases:
: * How much each selection contributes to an overall objective or target: For example, to specify how much a tactic contributes to different funnel stages.

* How an overall value or quantity should be split between selections: For example, to specify how the total time spent on an activity was divided between various distinct actions.

Procedure: Turn on Show Percentages for a Multi-Select List Attribute

### How the Show Percentages feature works

When you enable Show Percentages on a Multi-Select List attribute, the system initially sets the percentage values for all selected list options automatically: each time an additional list option is selected (or deselected), the system recalculates and adjusts the percentage value of each selection to maintain an equal distribution. For example, if there are four selections, each will be automatically assigned a value of 25% (1/4 of 100%).

After making all selections, users can manually adjust the percentage values for each selection as needed:

* The sum of all specified percentage values must always total exactly 100%.

* If the sum of the percentage values totals more or less than 100%, the system will display an error message. Any changes to the selections or their percentage values will not be saved until the user adjusts the percentage values to total exactly 100%.

* Users can enter percentage values as decimals, and can specify as many decimal places as they want. * Decimal places are also taken into account for the requirement that all percentage values must total 100%: if a decimal percentage value is specified for one selection, at least one other selection must also have a decimal percentage value with the same number of decimal places to sum to a whole number.

* Users can enter a value of 0% for any selection, including for multiple selections (provided the total of all values for all selections still equals 100%).

* After they are set, the percentage values are displayed beside the label of each selection wherever the attribute field is displayed.

Tip: Tip

The percentage values set on attribute selections can also be used by the Impact Modeler feature, for example to assign weighting on attribute-based scenarios. For details, see [Configure the Impact Modeler pipeline model](configure-impact-modeler.html).

## Deactivate and delete list options

You can deactivate or delete a list option at any time. Both deactivating and deleting a list option will make the list option no longer available as a selectable option on any activity where the attribute is used.

The difference between deactivating and deleting a list option is how the list option is handled on activities where it has been set as the selected value for the attribute:

* Deactivated: The list option is kept as the selected value on any activity where it was previously set (unless manually changed to a different list option).
