If you want to specify exactly how an investment's funds should be distributed between the connected activities, you can use the manual distribution method to set funds allocations by hand.

There are two options available for manual distribution:

* Set as amount: Set each connected activity's allocation by entering an exact currency amount.

* Set as percentage: Set each connected activity's allocation by entering it as a percentage of the total investment amount. ![Manual distribution amount percent comp](images/ManualDistributionAmountPercentComp.png)

Tip: Tips

In general, use manual distribution methods in cases where precise allocation amounts are important, and automatic distribution methods in cases where they are less important.

Distribute Equally is the least precise distribution method available, but also requires no manual effort. This method is useful when the exact allocation amounts are less important than tracking whether funds have been allocated.

* Recommended for: Plan budget columns.

Distribute Equally (only within date range) also requires no manual effort, but offers greater precision if your organization recognizes and tracks activity spend in relation to when activities are in-market.

* Recommended for: Expected budget columns.

Auto Distribution is the most precise automatic distribution method, because it's usually based on manually set allocations. It's useful when a budget column is expected to have very similar allocations to another column, but where the line item amount may be different.

* Recommended for: Committed and Actuals budget columns.

Set as percentage is the less precise of the manual distribution options. But it's also significantly faster (and requires less manual effort), because the system automatically calculates and allocates the correct currency amounts for you.

* Recommended for: Expected budget columns (if you need more precision than automatic distribution).

Set as amount gives you the most precise control, but also requires the most manual effort.

* Recommended for: Exceptions, when recording exact amounts is a priority.

## Change how an investment's funds are allocated

When you connect multiple activities to an investment, Uptempo will automatically distribute the investment's funds equally between all connected activities. If you want to adjust these allocations, you can use the Activity Funding Panel.

In the Activity Funding Panel, you can switch between the available distribution methods, and you can set funds allocation amounts if you select a manual distribution method.

Tip: Tip

The Activity Funding Panel is available on any investment line item that has at least one connected activity. It is not available for investment categories or sub-categories (even if an activity is connected), because you can't connect multiple activities to these investment types.

### Before you begin

To follow the instructions in this section:

* You must have access to the Investments section in your Uptempo instance.

* You must have access to all the activities connected to the investment for which you want to manually set funds allocations.

### Change the allocation distribution method for an investment line item

You can use an investment line item's Activity Funding Panel to view its current funds allocations, and to change the distribution method or adjust the allocations for each connected activity.

Note: Note

Investment line item funds are set per budget column, so allocation amounts for connected activities are also set individually for each budget column. As a result, each budget column has its own distribution method setting, and you set the distribution method separately for each column.

Procedure: Change the allocation distribution method

### Manually set allocation amounts for connected activities

If you want to set allocation amounts for connected activities yourself, you can use the Manual distribution method.

Procedure: Manually set funds allocations

### Automatically distribute funds only to in-market activities

By default, the Distribute Equally distribution method automatically allocates funds to all connected activities, even if they are not in-market for the time period that a budget column represents.

To make these automatic allocations reflect how and when activities need to be funded more accurately, you can configure the Distribute Equally method so that it only allocates funds to an activity if the activity's in-market dates overlap with the column's date range.

Example
: The budget column November Plan has a date range of November 1 - 30, 2026. The investment line item In-Person Events has four connected activities, with the following in-market dates:
: * Annual User Conference: May 14 - 16, 2026

* Regional Roadshow Event Denver: August 18, 2026

* Regional Roadshow Event Vancouver: November 5, 2026

* Fall Trade Show Booth: November 30 - December 3, 2026
: By default, all four connected activities are allocated an equal one-quarter (25%) share of the funds in the November Plan column. However, only two of the four activities have in-market dates that fall entirely or partly within the time period of November Plan: Regional Roadshow Event Vancouver (Nov 5) and Fall Trade Show Booth (Nov 30 - Dec 3).
: If you turn on the option to only allocate funds within the November Plan column's date range, the system automatically allocates an equal one-half (50%) share to only these two activities. It allocates no funds (0%) to the other two activities, as they are not relevant to the column's date range.
1

Procedure: Auto-distribute funds only to in-market activities



# What's new: 2025.10

Discover all the new Uptempo functionality introduced with the 2025.10 release, available from October 29, 2025.

* For details about bugs that have been fixed this month, see the [bug fixes page](bug-fixes-2025-10.html).

Note: Note

Features marked with the tags Preview and Beta are not activated by default, but can be turned on in your Uptempo instance on request.

For more information, see [Uptempo's feature release lifecycle](uptempo-s-feature-release-lifecycle.html).

## API: Marketing Planner

### New endpoints for elements and timelines

We have added new endpoints to the Marketing Planner API that enable bulk actions for elements and timelines. Developers can use these new capabilities to create multiple timelines or elements with a single call, saving time and enabling more efficient integrations with Marketing Planner.

→ [Learn more about the Marketing Planner API](https://developers.brandmaker.com/api/MAPS/8.0/8.0.1.0/)

## Campaign Management: Activity Configuration

### Support for "deny" access control statements

We've expanded the capabilities of the activity access control system in Uptempo Campaign Management to add support for the DENY effect in policy statements. Administrators can use this effect to create policies that explicitly prevent access, allowing for more precision and control when configuring access policies.

* More precision, less work: Previously, defining very granular access could often require a large number of very specific ALLOW statements. With the new DENY effect, you can now achieve the same outcome with less effort: grant access broadly with ALLOW, then use the DENY effect to precisely restrict access, reducing the overall number of access control statements you need to design and manage.

* Clearer intent and stronger security: Statements with a DENY effect always take priority, even over ALLOW statements with the same scope. This means you can use DENY statements to clearly implement mandatory "never allow" restrictions, and confidently ensure that edge cases are covered.

→ [Learn more about access controls for Uptempo Campaign Management](activity-access-control-config-overview.html)

### Retire KPIs that are no longer needed

Administrators can now deactivate Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) when they're no longer relevant.

* Remove KPIs without losing historical data: Deactivated KPIs are not shown on newly created activities, but remain visible on all previously created activities. This lets you evolve the way you track the performance of activities over time, without losing historical data. And, if you decide to start tracking a retired KPI again later, you can reactivate it at any time.

* Additional new capabilities for managing KPIs: To help administrators more easily manage their KPIs, it's now possible to change the display order of KPIs, and there is also a new search function to make it easier to find specific KPIs in the list.

→ [Learn more about creating and managing KPIs](creating-and-managing-key-performance-indicators-kpis.html)

### Track revenue for individual funnel stages in Impact Modeler

In the Impact Modeler, you can now track revenue for each funnel stage to get more insights into the pipeline created by your lead generation.

* Set up stage-specific revenue tracking: Administrators can turn on revenue tracking for any stage in the funnel, and set a custom label for any stage's revenue (such as "Pipeline", "Bookings", etc.).

* View revenue for a funnel stage: When revenue tracking is turned on for a funnel stage, stage-specific revenue is reflected in the Impact section of each activity's details panel:

* Planned tab: The funnel chart (under Show Funnel Assumptions) shows revenue projections (and the custom revenue labels) for each stage where they are enabled, in addition to the existing projection shown on the final stage:

![Impact revenue planned](images/ImpactRevenuePlanned.png)
* Actual tab: The input table for actuals now features columns (also using the custom revenue labels, and marked with the relevant currency symbol) where you can enter stage-specific revenue.

→ [Learn more about how to set up revenue-tracking per funnel stage in the Impact Modeler](configure-impact-modeler.html)

## Campaign Management: Activities

### Save Summary column configurations as presets

You can now save your custom column configurations in the Summary display mode as presets, so you can quickly apply them again at any time.

* Set up column presets for different tasks: With column presets, you don’t have to manually adjust the Summary table's columns every time you want to view different data. You can now create custom column setups tailored to your needs at each stage of the planning and execution cycle, and switch between them instantly with a single click. Plus, you can optionally link column presets to views, so that the right data columns are automatically displayed when you select a view.

* Create and manage shared presets for your organization: As an administrator, you can also create column presets that are available to all Uptempo Campaign Management users in your organization, and conveniently manage them in one place.

![Column presets](images/ColumnPresets.png)
→ [Learn more about how to use column presets in the Summary display mode](customize-summary-table.html)

### Redesigned Activities toolbar

We have redesigned the toolbar in the Activities section to make it work better on the most commonly used display sizes.

* Streamlined for usability: We tweaked the toolbar's design so that it takes up less space on the most common laptop and desktop display sizes, leaving more room for activity data. The text labels that appeared over some controls have been removed — instead, clearer and more consistent tooltip labels now appear when you hover on any button. Plus, the icons for some of the buttons have been updated to make their functions more intuitive.

* Grouping of functions: The toolbar controls are now visually grouped to indicate their relationships and functions. There is now a stronger visual link between controls that are related to the same function (such as the Select View menu and the View Options button). Sets of controls are now also thematically grouped based on whether they change the data that's displayed (search, filtering, etc.), add context to the visible data (grouping, color-coding, etc.), or apply only to the current display mode (for example column configuration in Summary or zoom in Timeline).

![New toolbar](images/NewToolbar.png)
→ [Learn more about the functions in the Activities toolbar](get-started-campaign.html)

### Improved workflow for deleting activities with connected investments

We have improved the way Campaign Management handles deleting activities with connected Financial Management investments to help prevent situations where investments are left unintentionally disconnected.

* Warning for connected investments: Whenever an activity with connected investments is deleted, Campaign Management now shows a warning dialog that lists the investments that will be disconnected as a result. To prevent accidental disconnection, users must explicitly confirm each investment to be disconnected before they can delete the activity.

* Copy a list of disconnected investments: To make it easier to reallocate the investments left disconnected after an activity is deleted, the warning dialog provides an option to copy the list of affected investments to the clipboard — users can simply paste the list into any text editing app for later reference.

### Color-code activities on the Timeline

In the Timeline display mode, you can now color-code the activity bars to visually identify activities.

* Set up color-coding for attributes: Administrators can configure color-coding on any Drop-Down List attribute by assigning a color to each attribute option.

* Display Timeline activities with color-coding: On the Timeline, you can use the new Select Color-Coding toolbar option to select an attribute, and the activity bars are color-coded according to the attribute list option set on each activity. Color-coding helps to visually declutter the Timeline, and makes it easier to interpret the visible activities at a glance.

![Timeline visuals color by2](images/TimelineVisualsColorBy2.png)
→ [Learn more about how to set up and use color-coding by attribute on the Timeline](configuring-color-coding-on-the-timeline.html)

### Connect multiple investments to an activity in a single step

When you connect investments to an activity from the Campaign Management side, you can now select multiple investments and connect them all to the activity with just one click.

The redesigned investment picker lets you select multiple investments to connect at once, saving you time and effort. You can even select investments across different plans and budgets, as the picker will remember all existing selections when you change the plan or budget you're viewing.

![Multi budget connection](images/MultiBudgetConnection.png)
→ [Learn more about connecting investments to activities](connect-investments-to-activities.html)

## Work: Assets

### Support for Fusion UX Logic custom web components in asset details

We've expanded support for Fusion UX Logic to Assets (formerly Media Pool), enabling you to add custom web components to the asset details view.

This new capability allows developers to create a new tab in the details view of all assets that displays a custom web component. You can use this feature to integrate relevant information from external systems (such as an asset's publication history) into the Assets user interface, putting it conveniently at your users' fingertips.

→ [Learn more about integrating custom components into Uptempo Work with Fusion UX Logic](https://developers.brandmaker.com/guides/fusion-ux-logic/)

## Work: Planner

### Enhancements to the element watch feature

Getting updates about changes to your marketing plan is now easier and more convenient with more options for watching elements:

* Watch an element from the context menu: Previously, you had to open an element's details page to watch it and receive update notifications. Now, you can quickly watch any element from its context menu: just right-click on an element in the Calendar or Budget views, then click Watch.

* Watch multiple elements with one click: Watching multiple elements is now also faster with a new bulk action. After you have selected multiple elements in the Calendar or Budget view, you can now right-click on the selected elements and click Watch to add all the selected elements to your watch list at once.

→ [Learn more about watching elements in Planner](https://download.brandmaker.com/doc/BM_help_en_100300000002/en-us/Content/03 Management/16-1 Beobachtung starten.htm)

### Support for sharing sorted views

We've added support for sorted views when sharing a link to an element in Planner.

Previously, if you were using a sorted view in the Calendar or Budget views, following a link to an element would reset the view to the Default View setting to highlight the linked element. Now, sorted views work with links to elements: if you open a link to an element while a sorted view is applied, the system will keep the sorted view active, and will highlight the first (topmost) instance of the linked element in the view.

→ [Learn more about sharing links to Planner elements](https://download.brandmaker.com/doc/BM_help_en_100300000002/en-us/Content/03 Management/14-6 Sicht teilen.htm)

### More precision and options for exchange rates

You now have even more control over exchange rates in Planner with new enhancements to the exchange rates settings (in Planner > Settings > Exchange Rates):

* Support for more precise exchange rates: When setting exchange rates in Planner, you can now enter rates with up to 9 decimal places (up from 4 previously), allowing for much greater precision in converted amounts.

* Automatically apply an edited rate to other months: When you edit the exchange rate for a particular month, you can now also apply the changed rate to other months at the same time, so you don't need to edit the other months manually. The Edit exchange rate dialog now has two new options that you can use to apply the new exchange rate to all remaining months in the same year, or retroactively to all months in previous years (or both). As before, the edited exchange rate will apply only to the selected month if you do not select either of these new options.

* Exchange rate month selection now defaults to FY starting month: On the Exchange Rates settings page, the month selection menu now defaults to the starting month of your fiscal year that was configured during your instance setup (instead of defaulting to January).

→ [Learn more about setting exchange rates in Planner](https://download.brandmaker.com/doc/BM_help_en_100300000001/en-us/Content/06 Administration/02 Einstellungen/A12-0 Währung Wechselkurs.htm)



# Bug fixes: 2025.10

To ensure that our users can benefit from bug fixes as soon as possible, we release bug fix updates to the Uptempo platform frequently.

The following bug fixes have been released over the past four weeks, between September 22 and October 24, 2025:

## API

* When saving Impact Actual data to a child activity of an activity that already has this type of data, the API no longer improperly returns a 500 Internal Server Error. Instead, it now correctly returns a 400 Bad Request error that specifies that the data can't be saved because the parent activity already contains Impact Actual data.

* When creating line items, the API no longer takes much more time to complete the request than expected (resulting in a timeout response in some cases).

## Campaign Management

* In attribute dependencies, changing the value of the controlling attribute will no longer cause an error to occur in cases where the dependent attribute already has a value that isn't valid for the new controlling attribute value.

* When an activity's details panel is open, the Open Comments button no longer covers the Log out button in the profile menu, preventing it from being clicked.

* When an activity's details panel is open, the Open Comments button no longer stays fixed in place when scrolling the details panel, but instead scrolls with other content.

* When the Comments panel for an activity is open, it no longer covers the tooltips of the buttons in the header bar, making them unreadable.

## Financial Management

* In the Funding Panel for allocating investment amounts to connected activities, the validation mechanism that checks for overallocated columns now only takes into account columns visible in the current view, preventing validation errors being shown for hidden columns (which can't be fixed from the Funding Panel).

* To avoid losing unsaved changes, the Funding Panel now prevents the selected view from being changed until all pending changes have been saved.

* Trying to create a line item for locked cells no longer causes an error.



# What's new: 2025.9

Discover all the new Uptempo functionality introduced with the 2025.9 release, available from September 24, 2025.

* For details about bugs that have been fixed this month, see the [bug fixes page](bug-fixes-2025-9.html).

* For notifications about functionality that is being removed from the platform, see the [deprecations and discontinuations page](deprecations-and-discontinuations-2025-9.html).

Note: Note

Features marked with the tags Preview and Beta are not activated by default, but can be turned on in your Uptempo instance on request.

For more information, see [Uptempo's feature release lifecycle](uptempo-s-feature-release-lifecycle.html).

## Uptempo Platform

### Unified navigation menu for Campaign Management, Financial Management & Work modules

To create a more cohesive and streamlined user experience across the Uptempo product, we have unified the sidebar navigation menus for the Campaign Management, Financial Management, and Work modules into a single navigation menu.

Previously, the sidebar navigation menu was split into two versions: one version for the Campaign Management/Financial Management modules, and a separate version for the Work modules. With the new unified navigation menu, you no longer need to switch between two separate menus. Instead, you can now seamlessly move between all Uptempo modules within a single menu, so you can get where you need to go faster and more easily.

### Self-service password resets

We've added the ability for users to reset their own Uptempo account passwords without an administrator's help. Previously, administrators had to manually reset user passwords, which created delays and added extra work. Now, users can simply click Forgot password? on the Uptempo login page to securely reset their own password.

* Faster and more convenient access: As a user, you no longer need to contact an administrator if you've forgotten your password. Just request a password reset to regain access to your account in minutes.

* Reduced workload: If you're an administrator, you save time and effort by no longer having to manage password resets.

* Secure reset process: Reset links expire after a set time, ensuring that your Uptempo accounts remain protected.

### Moving administration settings to Uptempo platform

To improve the user experience for Uptempo administrators, and to make the process of transitioning to the Uptempo platform easier and smoother for BrandMaker customers, we have migrated some system administration settings areas from the legacy BrandMaker platform to the Uptempo platform.

As part of this migration, we:

* Moved several settings pages out of the Administration > Overview section, and created new dedicated pages for them in the ![Administration](images/settings-2.svg) Administration section of the Uptempo navigation sidebar.

* Updated the moved settings pages to enhance their usability, and to give them a fresh look that matches the overall Uptempo platform design.

Note: Note

On all affected settings pages, no settings or functionality have been removed. Due to the redesign and reorganization of these pages, note that some settings have updated names, or are displayed in a different place than before.

→ [Learn more about which pages have moved, and how they have been updated](new-uptempo-administration-settings-pages.html)

## Campaign Management: Activity Configuration

### Set default activity color-coding on the Timeline

Continuing the Timeline Visuals enhancements introduced with Color by Attribute last month, you can now set default color-coding for activities in the Timeline based on their activity type or type group.

* Set colors for activity type groups: As an administrator, you can now assign colors to your activity type groups in the Activity Configuration section. You can also set colors directly on individual activity types if they're not part of a type group.

* Default color-coding on the Timeline: When an activity type group has been assigned a color, all activities belonging to that group are automatically displayed in that color on the Timeline. Color-coding by activity type group is used as the default setting for the Timeline (replacing the system-default purple) whenever Color By Attribute isn't active, making it easier for users to interpret the Timeline at a glance.

![Timeline visuals default color](images/TimelineVisualsDefaultColor.png)
→ [Learn more about setting up default Timeline Colors for activities](configuring-color-coding-on-the-timeline.html)

### More flexible rules for structuring the activity hierarchy

We've adjusted how activity rules work to add support for flexible hierarchy structures.

* Activity rules now allow multiple parents: With this update, you can now set up activity rules that specify more than one parent for any activity type or type group. In the past, activity rules only allowed an activity type (or type group) to have a single parent. This meant that any activity type could only be created under one specific parent type: for example, Tactic activities could only be created under Program activities, but never directly under Campaign activities. Now, any activity type or type group can have multiple parents — giving you much greater flexibility in the way you structure your hierarchy.

* Easier and faster hierarchy adjustments: In addition to giving you more design freedom, this change also significantly reduces your workload when you enter a new planning cycle. If you need to change the way your hierarchy works, you don't need to spend hours rebuilding it: now, you can easily make adjustments in minutes by only changing the activity types that need to change, while maintaining continuity with historical plans.

→ [Learn more about activity rules](create-activity-rules.html)

### Add formatted text content to activities

We've added a new attribute type for activities: with the Rich Text attribute type, you can now add fully-formatted text content to your activities.

* Make key context available on activities: The Rich Text attribute lets you provide detailed context about your marketing plans directly on your Uptempo activities. Now, you can add the full contents of your campaign one-pager (like a Plan On A Page or strategy document) directly to the relevant campaign activity so you can quickly reference it. Or, if you have related files (like slide decks or white papers) stored in the cloud, you can add clickable links to your activities to make those files easily accessible.

* Full support for essential text formatting options: The Rich Text attribute is displayed on activities as a WYSIWYG (What You See Is What You Get) text editor. Its toolbar lets you easily apply formatting to lay out and style your content: make text bold, italic, underlined, or a clickable link; add up to 3 levels of headings; organize text into numbered and bullet point lists; and highlight text with block quotes.

![Rich text attribute](images/RichTextAttribute.png)
→ [Learn more about setting up and using Rich Text attributes](activity-attribute-config-overview.html)

### Set which activity types can be connected to investments

If you connect your activities in Campaign Management to your investments in Financial Management, you can now set which types of activity can be connected to investments.

* Choose which activity types support Connect to Spend: As an administrator, you can now choose exactly which activity types can be connected to investments. If Connect to Spend is turned off for an activity type, activities of that type can't be connected to funding, and aren't shown as selectable options from the Financial Management side, either.

* Enforce bottom-up planning: Previously, it was possible to connect investments to activities at all levels of your activity hierarchy. If you use bottom-up planning in your organization, the ability to turn Connect to Spend on or off by activity type means you can now ensure that team members are only connecting investments to the bottom level of your activity hierarchy. This creates greater clarity in your planning and reporting, and means you can spend less time fixing incorrectly connected funds.

→ [Learn more about configuring Connect to Spend on activity types](create-and-configure-activity-types.html)

### Deactivate list options that are no longer needed

You can now deactivate any list option on Drop-Down List or Multi-Select List attributes — even if it is currently in use on activities.

* Declutter list options: After you deactivate a list option, it can no longer be selected on any new or existing activity. This is useful for adjusting the list options on attributes that need to be updated routinely: for example, an attribute that specifies the person responsible for reviewing or approving an activity.

* Preserves historical data: If a deactivated list option was previously selected on any existing activities, it stays in place on those activities so that you can still reference historical data.

→ [Learn more about managing list options for attributes](https://knowledge.uptempo.io/doc/BM_Uptempo_en_109100000000/Content/06 Administration/01 Attributes/A2-03 ConfigAttr.htm#ListOptions)

### Make dependent attributes conditionally required

Attribute dependencies now support required dependent attributes.

* Set any dependent attribute as required: Before, you could not use an attribute as a dependent attribute if it was required on any activity type. That restriction is now gone, and you can now set dependent attributes as required.

* Create activities faster with conditionally required dependent attributes: In dependency chains of multiple linked attributes, you can make dependent attributes conditionally required: they're required only when specific options are selected in the controlling attribute, but otherwise don't need to be filled out. Together with this release's other improvements to attribute dependencies, this allows you to build streamlined dynamic forms in the activity details panel that significantly reduce the time needed to create an activity.

![Required dependent attribute](images/RequiredDependentAttribute.png)
→ [Learn more about setting up attribute dependencies](attribute-dependencies.html)

## Campaign Management: Activities

### Improved column configuration for Summary

We've completely redesigned the controls for configuring columns in the Summary display mode. The number of available columns has increased with recent releases, so we've made some improvements to make it easier to navigate the longer list of available columns. Highlights of the redesign include:

* Separate list of visible columns: Columns that are selected to be displayed in the table are now shown in their own, separate list. This lets you see at a glance which columns are currently selected, and makes managing your displayed columns faster.

* Grouping and search: Finding the exact column you want is now much easier: the list of available columns is organized into column groups that have their own subheadings, and we've also added a search function.

* Pinned columns: You can now select up to 3 columns to "pin". Like the Activities column, pinned columns always stay visible when you scroll the Summary table side-to-side.

![Column picker new updated](images/ColumnPickerNewUpdated.png)
→ [Learn more about the new column configuration menu](customize-summary-table.html)

### Comments on activities

You can now add comments directly to activities in Campaign Management, making it easier to collaborate in context.

* Keep communication in one place: With the new comments feature in Uptempo Campaign Management, you can capture communication about your marketing activities right on the activities themselves. Use comments to add explanations of updates and record decisions, so that the reasoning behind changes and approvals is always clear and visible — no more digging through chats, emails, and meeting notes to figure out what happened.

* Keep everyone in the loop: When an activity needs someone's attention, you can @mention them in comments, and they'll see a notification in Uptempo. Plus, you can reply directly to a comment to create a thread and keep discussions organized.

* React with emoji: For those times words when words aren't quite enough (or when a thumbs-up is all you need), emoji are supported both in comment text, and as reactions.

![Activity comments](images/ActivityComments.png)
→ [Learn more about activity comments](commenting-on-activities.html)

### User experience enhancements for attribute dependencies

We've made several UX improvements in the details panel to make it easier and faster to work with attribute dependencies, and to support required dependent attributes:

* Grouped attribute controls for dependencies: If an activity type includes one or more attribute dependencies, the details panel now groups all attributes that are part of a dependency into their own section, and visually shows how the attributes are linked to one another. Within this section, the system does not auto-save attribute values as soon as they are selected. Instead, values are only saved when you click Save: this helps to protect data integrity by ensuring that all required dependent attributes have a value selected before changes are saved in the system. ![Required dependent attribute2](images/RequiredDependentAttribute2.png)

* Dynamically hidden dependent attribute fields: In chained dependencies that contain multiple linked attributes, the system now automatically hides all dependent attributes until they're needed. For example, if a conditionally required dependent attribute is only valid for certain values in the controlling attribute, it is initially not shown in the details panel: it only becomes visible after you select one of the controlling attribute values that make it required. This simplifies the details panel, and makes it easier to use for activity types with a lot of attributes.

* Auto-select dependent attribute values: The system now saves you unnecessary clicks when a dependent attribute only has one possible value. Whenever you choose an option in a controlling attribute that leaves only one valid option in its dependent attribute, the system simply selects that option for you — no extra click needed.

→ [Learn more about attribute dependencies](attribute-dependencies.html)

## Financial Management: Investments

### Connect multiple activities to a line item in a single step

Connecting multiple Campaign Management activities to a Financial Management investment line item is now faster and easier. You no longer need to connect activities to a line item one-by-one: in the redesigned activity picker, you can select multiple activities at once, then connect them all to the line item with a single click.

![Multi activity connection](images/MultiActivityConnection.png)
→ [Learn more about connecting activities to investments](https://knowledge.uptempo.io/doc/BM_Uptempo_en_109200000000/Content/02 Planung/Doku/05-3-6 ConnectActivitiesInvest.htm)



# Bug fixes: 2025.9

To ensure that our users can benefit from bug fixes as soon as possible, we release bug fix updates to the Uptempo platform frequently.

The following bug fixes have been released over the past four weeks, between August 25 and September 19, 2025:

## API

* When retrieving activities from the API, using the filtering function no longer causes a timeout error to occur.

* When an API user resets their Uptempo account password, this no longer causes them to be unable to authenticate into the API.

## Campaign Management

* Updating an attribute dependency no longer causes an error to occur when a list-type attribute in the dependency has deactivated list options.

* It is no longer possible to create duplicate activity rules (which prevents further rules from being created).

* Activity rules that have been deactivated are now ignored when checking if they would conflict with adding an attribute to an activity type.

* The inherited attributes section of the activity details panel no longer shows blank lines for attributes that have been deleted.

* When a view is selected on the Timeline that includes a selected Color By option, changing the Color By setting to a different option and reloading the page no longer causes the Color By setting to revert to the option saved in the view.

* The color picker for setting Timeline Visuals (Color By) colors no longer includes the "white" color preset as an available option, as activity bars set to this color are not clearly visible on the Timeline.

* In the Filter by menu, the arrow on the Select Attribute menu is no longer unresponsive to clicking, but instead opens the menu as expected.

* In the View Options menu, the Save button is now inactive when no view name has been entered, to prevent the error that occurs when trying to save a view with no name specified.

## Financial Management

* For line items connected to activities where Auto Allocation is active, the system no longer fails to save changes to column amounts in the Funding Panel (despite indicating that changes have been saved).

* After deleting one or more line items with values in at least one currency column, taking a snapshot of the currency column no longer causes an error to occur.

* The Transfer Requests page should now take less time to load.

* Editing an import schedule at the same time as another user no longer causes an error to occur when saving changes.



# Deprecations and discontinuations: 2025.9

From time to time, we must remove functionality from the Uptempo platform for various reasons. To give users time to prepare and adjust, we communicate functionality removals in two stages:

1. Deprecation: Deprecated functionality is still available in the Uptempo platform as of the current release, but is planned to be removed with a future release. Deprecated functionality usually does not receive any further updates, and only remains available to already existing users.

2. Discontinuation: Discontinued functionality has been removed from the Uptempo platform as of the current release, and is no longer available.

If functionality that you use is announced as deprecated on this page, we recommend that you begin preparing for its removal as soon as possible to ensure a smooth transition.

## Deprecations

With this release, the following functionality has been deprecated:

None this month.

## Discontinuations

With this release, the following functionality has been discontinued:

### Support for Chinese, Japanese, Korean, Norwegian, Russian as Uptempo Standard Languages

As announced in the July and 2025.8 releases, support for the following user interface (UI) languages has now been discontinued with the 2025.9 release:

* Chinese

* Japanese

* Korean

* Norwegian

* Russian

These languages were previously included as part of the Uptempo Standard Languages, which means that Uptempo regularly provided and maintained translations of product UI text as part of ongoing releases.

Note: Note

You can manage UI languages in Uptempo under Administration > Overview > Look & Feel > UI Languages. These UI language settings apply to Uptempo Work modules, but not to Uptempo Campaign Management or Financial Management.

#### What’s changed

Support for the affected languages has officially ended with the 2025.9 release on September 24, 2025. On this date:

* If you had one or more of these languages enabled in your Uptempo instance, the affected languages were automatically converted to custom languages. This means:

* These languages were moved from the Uptempo Standard Languages section to the Your Custom Languages section.

* The moved languages retained all translations provided by Uptempo as of the 2025.7 release, but will no longer receive updates from Uptempo.

* You can continue using and extending these translations yourself, just like any other custom language.

* If an affected language was not enabled in your Uptempo instance, it was removed entirely, and not migrated to the Your Custom Languages section.

* The exception to this is if an affected language was not enabled, but had been modified using the Customize function. In this case, the customized language was automatically moved to the Your Custom Languages section.

#### Need access to past translations?

If you would like to use an existing Uptempo-created translation after September 24, 2025, Uptempo will provide the translation data source file upon request (with all Uptempo-provided translations as of the 2025.7 release). You can upload this file to create a new custom language in your instance.

If you have any questions or would like to request a translation file, please contact your Uptempo customer success manager, or reach out to our support team.



# Announcement: New Uptempo administration settings pages

## What's new & changed

We've migrated several settings pages from the legacy BrandMaker platform to the Uptempo platform. To do this, we've moved the affected settings pages out of the Administration > Overview section, and moved them into four new pages in Uptempo's Administration menu:

* Mailing & Templates Service

* UI Languages

* Uptempo Settings

* Appearance Settings

![Admin new pages](images/AdminNewPages.png)
As part of migrating these pages, we also reworked their design to match the overall style and color scheme of the Uptempo platform, and made several user experience improvements, including reorganizing the page layouts for easier navigation.

Here's an overview of the new Uptempo administration settings pages and what's changed on each one:

### Mailing & Templates Service

Previous names: E-mail Templates and E-mail Service

Previous locations: Administration > Overview > Look & Feel > E-mail Templates and Administration > Overview > System Configuration > E-mail Service

What's on this page: Settings related to email notifications that the Uptempo platform can send to users when certain system events happen.

What's changed: Combines two related settings pages into a single page for convenience. Redesigned and reorganized for improved usability.

![Admin mailing templates](images/AdminMailingTemplates.png)

### UI Languages

Previous name: UI Languages (no change)

Previous location: Administration > Overview > Look & Feel > UI Languages

What's on this page: Settings to select and customize the user interface languages of Uptempo Work modules.

What's changed: Redesigned and reorganized for improved usability. Some former default languages have been removed as previously announced (see the [deprecations and discontinuations page](deprecations-and-discontinuations-2025-9.html)for details).

![Admin ui languages](images/AdminUILanguages.png)

### Uptempo Settings

Previous name: System Settings

Previous location: Administration > Overview > System Configuration > System Settings

What's on this page: General system settings and module-specific settings for Uptempo Work modules (including legacy modules).

What's changed: Completely redesigned and reorganized, with all settings now grouped into subsections for improved navigation. All settings now also have user-friendly names and a brief description to make them easier to understand and use.

![Admin uptempo settings](images/AdminUptempoSettings.png)

### Appearance Settings

Previous name: Appearance Settings (in some cases Desktop Skinning)

Previous location: Administration > Overview > Look & Feel > Appearance Settings

What's on this page: Settings to customize the visual design of the Uptempo user interface.

What's changed: Redesigned and reorganized for improved usability.

![Admin appearance settings](images/AdminAppearanceSettings.png)



# What's new: 2025.8

Discover all the new Uptempo functionality introduced with the 2025.8 release, available from August 27, 2025.

* For details about bugs that have been fixed this month, see the [bug fixes page](bug-fixes-2025-8.html).

* For notifications about functionality that is planned to be retired, see the [deprecations and discontinuations page](deprecations-and-discontinuations-2025-8.html).

Note: Note

Features marked with the tags Preview and Beta are not activated by default, but can be turned on in your Uptempo instance on request.

For more information, see [Uptempo's feature release lifecycle](uptempo-s-feature-release-lifecycle.html).

## Uptempo Platform

### Improved login experience for SSO users

We have adjusted the Uptempo sign-in process to provide a better user experience, in particular for organizations that use Single Sign-On (SSO). In the past, if you tried to sign in to Uptempo while you still had an active session (for example, because you are already logged in to your SSO service), you were taken to a page that said that you were already signed in. To streamline the sign-in process, we have eliminated this page: now, if you sign in to Uptempo with an active session, you'll simply be redirected to the Uptempo application immediately.

## Administration

### User experience enhancements in the User List

To improve the user experience for Uptempo administrators, we've implemented some tweaks in the User List:

* Sort by status: The Status column in the User List is now sortable. As an administrator, you can use this capability to more easily find deactivated or locked user accounts to restore their access.

* Last Activity is now Last Login: Until now, the timestamp in the Last Activity column of the User List indicated the last date on which there was any modification to the user account itself (such as a change in the account's status or profile information). To make this data more useful to administrators, we have renamed the column to Last Login, and the timestamp now records the date each user last successfully signed in. As a result, it's now much simpler to identify and remove inactive accounts.

→ [Learn more about managing users in an Uptempo instance](https://knowledge.uptempo.io/doc/BM_help_en_100100000001/en-us/Content/Administration/Topics/Benutzerverwaltung.htm)

## Campaign Management: Activity Configuration

### Retire KPIs that are no longer needed

Administrators can now deactivate Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) when they're no longer relevant.

* Deactivated KPIs are not shown on newly created activities, but remain visible on all previously created activities. This lets you evolve the way you track the performance of activities over time, without losing historical data. And, if you decide to start tracking a retired KPI again later, you can reactivate it at any time.

* As part of this release, we've also added a few new capabilities to help administrators manage KPIs: you can now change the display order of KPIs, and there is also a new search function to make it easier to find specific KPIs in the list.

→ [Learn more about the new KPI administration features](creating-and-managing-key-performance-indicators-kpis.html)

### Track revenue for individual funnel stages

In the Impact Modeller, you can now track revenue for each funnel stage to get more insights into the pipeline created by your lead generation. Administrators can turn on revenue tracking for any stage in the funnel, and set a custom label for any stage's revenue (such as "Pipeline", "Bookings", etc.). When revenue tracking is turned on for a funnel stage, stage-specific revenue is reflected in the Impact section of each activity's details panel:

* Planned tab: The funnel chart (under Show Funnel Assumptions) shows revenue projections (and the custom revenue labels) for each stage where they are enabled, in addition to the existing projection shown on the final stage: ![Impact revenue planned](images/ImpactRevenuePlanned.png)

* Actual tab: The input table for actuals now features columns (also using the custom revenue labels, and marked with the relevant currency symbol) where you can enter stage-specific revenue.

→ [Learn more about tracking revenue per funnel stage](configure-impact-modeler.html)

## Campaign Management: Activities

### Visually differentiate activities in Timeline

In the Timeline display mode, you can now color-code the activity bars to visually identify activities.

* Administrators can configure Color By Attribute on any Drop-Down List attribute by assigning a color to each attribute option.

* On the Timeline, users can select an attribute where Color By Attribute is set up with the new Color by control, and the activity bars change color according to the option set on each activity.

* The Color By Attribute feature helps to visually declutter the Timeline, and makes it easier for users to interpret the visible activities at a glance.

![Timeline visuals color by2](images/TimelineVisualsColorBy2.png)
→ [Learn more about configuring and using Color By Attribute](color-coding-timeline-activities.html)

### Connect multiple investments to an activity in a single step

Connecting multiple Financial Management investments to a Campaign Management activity is now faster and easier. Similar to the enhancement we made on the Financial Management side in last month's release, when you connect investments to an activity from the Campaign Management side, you can now select multiple investments and connect them all to the activity with a single click.

![Multi budget connection](images/MultiBudgetConnection.png)
→ [Learn more about connecting investments to activities](connect-investments-to-activities.html)

### Improved column configuration for Summary

We've redesigned the controls for configuring columns in the Summary display mode. The number of available columns has increased with recent releases, so we've made some improvements to make it easier to navigate the longer list of available columns. Highlights of the redesign include:

* Separate list of visible columns: Columns that are selected to be displayed in the table are now shown in their own, separate list. This lets you see at a glance which columns are currently visible, and quickly make changes.

* Grouping and search: Finding the exact column you want just got easier: the list of available columns is now organized into column groups that have their own subheadings, and there's also a search function to find columns by keyword.

* Pinned columns: You can now "pin" up to 3 columns. Like the fixed Activities column, pinned columns always remain visible when you scroll the Summary table side-to-side.

![Column picker new](images/ColumnPickerNew.png)
Note: Note

If you want to try out this beta feature and share your feedback with us, please reach out to your Customer Success contact.

### Save activity hierarchy display settings as views

When you use display settings (such as filters and groupings) to customize which activities are shown in the Activity Hierarchy, you can now save these settings as views.

* After you save a view, you can select it from the new Views menu in the Timeline and Summary to apply the same display settings with just one click.

* Any user with access to the Activities section can create their own personal views that aren't visible to other users, either from scratch or by copying an existing view. Administrators can additionally create views that can be used by all users in their organization.

![Saved views example](images/SavedViewsExample.png)
→ [Learn more about creating and using saved views](save-activity-hierarchy-display-settings-as-views.html)

### View inherited attribute values in activity details

For any activity, you can now see exactly which attributes it has inherited from activities above it in the hierarchy, and what their values are. In Uptempo, activities have both their own attributes, and attributes that they "inherit" from their ancestor activities (parents, grandparents, etc.). Whenever you view any activity, the activity's Details section now also lists every ancestor activity from which attributes are being inherited: expand any listed ancestor activity to see all the attributes inherited from it, along with their values.

Note: Note

For inherited attributes, the ancestor activity names, inherited attribute names, and the attribute values respect all applicable access controls. As a result, users will only see the details that they have permission to view.

![Inherited attributes](images/InheritedAttributes.png)
→ [Learn more about activity attributes and inheritance](configure-attribute-list-options.html)

## Financial Management: Investments

### Calculated columns in Activity Funding Panel

The Activity Funding Panel now displays calculated columns. Previously, if a Financial Management View contained calculated columns, these columns weren't visible when allocating funds from investments to connected activities. Now, if you select a View in the Activity Funding Panel that includes calculated columns, those columns are shown in the panel. Just like elsewhere in Uptempo Financial Management, calculated columns in the Activity Funding Panel are marked with a ![Calculated](images/calculator-1.svg) Calculated icon by their column name, and their contents can't be edited.

![Calculated columns funding panel](images/CalculatedColumnsFundingPanel.png)
→ [Learn more about using the Activity Funding Panel to allocate connected funds](https://knowledge.uptempo.io/doc/BM_Uptempo_en_109200000000/Content/02 Planung/Doku/05-3-6 ConnectActivitiesInvest.htm#Manually)

### Automatically distribute funds allocations between connected activities

You can now configure specific Uptempo Financial Management columns to automatically distribute funds between connected activities.

The new Auto Distribution feature eliminates the need to do manual data entry for allocations — especially for funds amounts imported into Uptempo from integrations (such as Committed and Actual amounts). When Auto Distribution is configured for a Financial Management column, the system will automatically allocate the line item amount in that column to connected activities in the same proportions as a specified reference column. For example, if a line item's funds in the "Expected Spend" column are split 70/30 between two activities, and you set this column as the reference for the line item's "Actual Spend" column, any amount entered in the "Actual Spend" column will also be automatically split 70/30 between the same two connected activities.

![Auto distribution percentages](images/AutoDistributionPercentages.png)
* In conjunction with Auto Distribution, we've also added a UX enhancement to the Activity Funding Panel: now, if a Date Range is set for a Financial Management column, cells where the column's date range overlaps with a connected activity's in-market dates are highlighted with a colored background to show at a glance where funds need to be allocated. ![Allocation date range highlighting](images/AllocationDateRangeHighlighting.png)

→ [Learn more about setting up and using Auto Distribution](https://knowledge.uptempo.io/doc/BM_Uptempo_en_109200000000/Content/02 Planung/Doku/05-3-6-2 AutoDistribution.htm)



# Bug fixes: 2025.8

To ensure that our users can benefit from bug fixes as soon as possible, we release bug fix updates to the Uptempo platform frequently.

The following bug fixes have been released over the past four weeks, between July 28 and August 22, 2025:

## Campaign Management

* When an attribute option on a Multi-Select List attribute is deactivated:

* It is no longer shown as an available option when selecting attribute options (if it is not currently selected).

* It can now be removed as a selected option on attributes where it was selected before being deactivated.

* It will no longer accept inputs in the percentage field if Percentage Allocation is also enabled on the attribute.

* The Summary display mode no longer experiences a performance degradation in cases where there are a large number of activities that have Multi-Select List attributes with Percentage Allocation enabled.

* On the Timeline, after you use Group by on an attribute that results in some activities being in the group "No value set", using the quick search function no longer causes an error.

* On activities with two distinct attribute dependency chains, editing both of the dependency chains at the same time but then canceling one of the changes no longer causes the action buttons to disappear from both dependency chains.

* When you rename a view to a name that already has one or more copies (for example renaming a view to "Example View" when "Example View (copy)", "Example View (copy 2)", etc. already exist), the system will no longer unnecessarily auto-append "(copy X)" to the name in cases where the specified name is available (for example if the original "Example View" was deleted or renamed).

* In the View options menu, a particular sequence of actions no longer causes the system to incorrectly display the "Save as new" and "Save" buttons as deactivated when they should be active.

* The system now handles switching between Campaign Management and Financial Management better when a view is applied to the Campaign Management Timeline or Summary. If you click on a link to a connected activity from an investment on the Financial Management side, and a view is still applied on the Campaign Management side that does not display that activity, Campaign Management now automatically switches back to the "All activities" view to ensure the activity can be displayed.

* Connecting an investment line item an activity no longer causes that line item to be displayed four times in the activity's details panel.

* The investment picker ("Select investment to connect to...") dialog now has a fixed size to improve usability, and avoid having to scroll past the bottom of the screen.

## Financial Management

* To ensure that Campaign Management access controls are fully applied when a user connects an activity to an investment, the system now also evaluates all attributes attached to the activity against the user's applicable access control policies.



# Deprecations and discontinuations: 2025.8

From time to time, we must remove functionality from the Uptempo platform for various reasons. To give users time to prepare and adjust, we communicate functionality removals in two stages:

1. Deprecation: Deprecated functionality is still available in the Uptempo platform as of the current release, but is planned to be removed with a future release. Deprecated functionality usually does not receive any further updates, and only remains available to already existing users.

2. Discontinuation: Discontinued functionality has been removed from the Uptempo platform as of the current release, and is no longer available.

If functionality that you use is announced as deprecated on this page, we recommend that you begin preparing for its removal as soon as possible to ensure a smooth transition.

## Deprecations

With this release, the following functionality has been deprecated:

### Legacy User Group Endpoints in Marketing Planner API

To support a more scalable, maintainable, and consistent platform, we’ve migrated Marketing Planner API endpoints related to user group management to a newer, more robust module. As part of this transition, we are deprecating legacy endpoints that are now redundant or inconsistent with our updated architecture.

#### What's changing

The following legacy endpoints are now deprecated:

* All methods for the UserGroupsRestApi (`/usergroups`) endpoint

* The following methods for the GroupRestApi (`/group`) endpoint: * `POST_createGroup` * `PUT_editGroup` * `DELETE_deleteGroup`

#### What this means

* These endpoints are now marked as deprecated in API documentation and API responses

* API responses for these endpoints now include deprecation headers

We encourage all developers and integrators to begin migrating to the new user group management endpoints as soon as possible.

For more details, please refer to our [Marketing Planner API documentation](https://developers.brandmaker.com/api/MAPS/8.0/8.0.1.0/).

### Update: End of Support for Chinese, Japanese, Korean, Norwegian, Russian as Uptempo Standard Languages

With the 2025.7 release, we announced the deprecation of support for the following user interface (UI) languages:

* Chinese

* Japanese

* Korean

* Norwegian

* Russian

These languages were previously included as part of the Uptempo Standard Languages, which means that Uptempo regularly provided and maintained translations of product UI text as part of ongoing releases.

The affected languages were initially planned to be discontinued with the 2025.8 release. To give our users more time to make this transition, we have decided to postpone the discontinuation to the 2025.9 release.

Note: Note

You can manage UI languages in Uptempo under Administration > Overview > Look & Feel > UI Languages. These UI language settings apply to Uptempo Work modules, but not to Uptempo Campaign Management or Financial Management.

#### What’s changing

Support for the affected languages will officially end with the 2025.9 release on September 24, 2025. At that time:

* If you have one or more of these languages enabled in your Uptempo instance the affected languages will be automatically converted to custom languages. This means:

* These languages will be moved from the Uptempo Standard Languages section to the Your Custom Languages section.

* The moved languages will retain all translations provided by Uptempo as of the 2025.7 release, but will no longer receive updates from Uptempo.

* You can continue using and extending these translations yourself, just like any other custom language.

* If an affected language is not enabled in your Uptempo instance, it will be removed entirely, and not migrated to the Your Custom Languages section.

* The exception to this is if an affected language is not enabled, but has been modified using the Customize function. In this case, the language will be treated as if it were enabled, and will be automatically moved to the Your Custom Languages section.

* New customers whose Uptempo instance is activated after July 30, 2025 will not see the discontinued languages in either section by default.

#### Need access to past translations?

If you would like to use an existing Uptempo-created translation after the discontinuation, Uptempo will provide the translation data source file upon request (with all Uptempo-provided translations as of the 2025.7 release). You can upload this file to create a new custom language in your instance.

If you have any questions or would like to request a translation file, please contact your Uptempo customer success manager, or reach out to our support team.

## Discontinuations

With this release, the following functionality has been discontinued:

None this month.



# What's new: 2025.7

Discover all the new Uptempo functionality introduced with the 2025.7 release, available from July 30, 2025.

* For details about bugs that have been fixed this month, see the [bug fixes page](bug-fixes-2025-7.html).

* For notifications about functionality that is planned to be retired, see the [deprecations and discontinuations page](deprecations-and-discontinuations-2025-7.html).

Note: Note

Features marked with the tags Preview and Beta are not activated by default, but can be turned on in your Uptempo instance on request.

For more information, see [Uptempo's feature release lifecycle](uptempo-s-feature-release-lifecycle.html).

## Announcement: Updates to the Uptempo feature release lifecycle

We are making some changes to our feature release lifecycle. These changes are designed to improve transparency, and to help our customers plan and prepare for upcoming product changes more effectively.

In brief, we are:

* Renaming the Early Access stage to Beta to more clearly communicate the in-development nature of these features.

* Adding a new Preview stage to give you time to evaluate new Uptempo features before they are automatically rolled out to all customers.

* Publishing the details of our full feature release lifecycle (from initial availability to discontinuation), so you know exactly what to expect at each stage.

Here's what these changes mean for you:

* Major new Uptempo Campaign Management & Financial Management features will be released in Preview first: Features in Preview are available on an opt-in basis. This means you can choose exactly when you are ready to start using them. * Minor enhancements (such as UX/UI and performance improvements) will continue to be released directly to General Availability, and will not be released into the Preview stage first.

* Preview features are complete and ready to use: Unlike Beta (formerly Early Access) features, Preview features are not actively evolving, and come with full support and documentation.

* Features in Preview automatically transition to General Availability: Preview features are made generally available after 2 monthly release cycles. This means that new features are automatically turned on for all customers approximately 2 months after they are first released in Preview. * For example, a feature released to Preview in the May release will become generally available on the release date for the July release (typically the last Wednesday of the month).

Initially, we are releasing only certain features to Preview. All other features will continue to be released directly to General Availability. At a later date (to be announced), we will transition to releasing all major new Campaign Management & Financial Management features to Preview first.

Just like Beta features, we'll announce Preview features in the release notes with a special tag. Going forward, we'll also announce in the release notes each month which Preview features have moved into General Availability with the release.

For more information about Uptempo's product release lifecycle and what to expect, see our detailed overview:

[Learn more about how we release new features to Uptempo products](uptempo-s-feature-release-lifecycle.html)

## Campaign Management: Activity Configuration

### Support for conditionally required dependent attributes

Attribute dependencies now support required dependent attributes. Before, you could not use an attribute as a dependent attribute if it was required on any activity type. This update removes that restriction, so that you can now make any dependent attribute required. In dependency chains of multiple linked attributes, this means you can make dependent attributes conditionally required: they're required only after certain values are selected in the controlling attribute. Together with this release's other improvements to attribute dependencies, this change makes it possible to build dynamic, streamlined forms in the activity Details panel that significantly reduce the time needed to create an activity.

![Required dependent attribute](images/RequiredDependentAttribute.png)
[Learn more about these attribute dependency enhancements](attribute-dependencies.html)

## Campaign Management: Activities

### User experience enhancements for attribute dependencies

We've made several UX improvements in the details panel to make it easier and faster to work with attribute dependencies, and to support required dependent attributes:

* Grouped attribute controls for dependencies: If an activity type includes one or more attribute dependencies, the details panel now groups all attributes that are part of a dependency into their own section, and visually shows how the attributes are linked to one another. Within this section, the system does not auto-save attribute values as soon as they are selected. Instead, values are only saved when you click Save: this helps to protect data integrity by ensuring that all required dependent attributes have a value selected before changes are saved in the system. ![Required dependent attribute2](images/RequiredDependentAttribute2.png)

* Dynamically hidden dependent attribute fields: In chained dependencies that contain multiple linked attributes, the system now automatically hides all dependent attributes until they're needed. For example, if a conditionally required dependent attribute is only valid for certain values in the controlling attribute, it is initially not shown in the details panel: it only becomes visible after you select one of the controlling attribute values that make it required. This simplifies the details panel, and makes it easier to use for activity types with a lot of attributes.

* Auto-select dependent attribute values: The system now saves you unnecessary clicks when a dependent attribute only has one possible value. Whenever you choose an option in a controlling attribute that leaves only one valid option in its dependent attribute, the system simply selects that option for you — no extra click needed.

[Learn more about these attribute dependency enhancements](attribute-dependencies.html)

## Financial Management: Investments

### Connect multiple activities to a line item in a single step

Connecting multiple Campaign Management activities to a Financial Management investment line item is now faster and easier. You no longer need to connect activities to a line item one-by-one: in the redesigned activity picker, you can select multiple activities at once, then connect them all to the line item with a single click.

![Multi activity connection](images/MultiActivityConnection.png)
[Learn more about connecting activities and investments](https://knowledge.uptempo.io/doc/BM_Uptempo_en_109200000000/Content/02 Planung/Doku/05-3-6 ConnectActivitiesInvest.htm)

### Improved filtering capabilities for tracking activity funding

We have added more filtering options to the investment hierarchy table to give you better insight into activity funding and funds allocation. Before, you could filter the table to see only investments that are connected to activities. With the new and expanded Filter by Activity Funding options, you can now also see the specific amounts allocated to connected activities. And the opposite view is now also available, so you can see investment amounts that are not yet allocated to any activity. These new filter options provide a more complete view of how your investments support activities, helping you to manage budgets and make informed decisions.

![Filter by activity funding updated](images/FilterByActivityFundingUpdated.png)
[Learn more about filtering investments by activity funding](https://knowledge.uptempo.io/doc/BM_Uptempo_en_109200000000/Content/03 Management/06-5a FilterActivityFunding.htm)



# Bug fixes: 2025.7

To ensure that our users can benefit from bug fixes as soon as possible, we release bug fix updates to the Uptempo platform frequently.

The following bug fixes have been released over the past four weeks, between June 23 and July 25, 2025:

## All Modules

### Navigation

* The sidebar navigation menu now displays animated "placeholders" while it is loading menu items to signal that it is actively loading.

## Administration

### Look & Feel

* When you customize the system colors in Look & Feel > Desktop Skinning, the Highlight color setting no longer excludes the Expand and Collapse buttons in the navigation sidebar (which would always default to white).

## API

### Campaign Management API

* Fetching all activity types with the API in an instance where one attribute is set at the controlling attribute for more than one dependent attribute no longer causes an error to be returned instead of the requested activity types.

## Campaign Management

### Activity Configuration

* When an access control statement gives a user access to an activity through a condition based on attributes, the system no longer denies access when the user tries to modify the attributes on that activity.

* Deleting an option from an attribute of the type Multi-Select List no longer causes an error to occur.

* For attribute dependencies where two or more controlling attributes each have more than one dependent attribute, setting the value for a dependent attribute no longer causes an error to occur.

* For attribute dependency chains, the attributes in the chain no longer fail to appear in the activity setup assistant when the first controlling attribute in the chain is not enabled on the activity type being created.

### Activities

* It is no longer possible to clear the values on required attributes of the type Drop-Down List or Multi-Select List, which would cause the details panel to enter a state in which attribute values couldn't be saved.

* The list of selected filter options is no longer cut off when a large number of filter options are selected in the Filter by menu.

* Selecting exactly 36 options on a Multi-Select List attribute with percentage allocation enabled no longer causes the details panel to enter a state in which attribute values couldn't be saved.

* Selecting a controlling attribute value and then clearing it no longer causes an error to occur when you create a new activity of a type that has an attribute dependency chain.

* For a dependent attribute that is a Multi-Select List attribute where percentage allocation is enabled, selecting a different controlling attribute option no longer causes the auto-calculated percentage allocations to be incorrect when a selected dependent attribute value is removed (because it is no longer valid for the new controlling attribute option).

* In the Timeline display mode, an activity's timeline bar is no longer misaligned until the screen is refreshed when you change the activity's start date in the details panel.

* When using the Add under function to create a new activity, the activity setup assistant no longer displays a blank window when go back to the Details step after reaching the KPI step.

* When the Impact Modeller feature is not enabled, navigating to the Impact > Actual tab on an activity no longer causes an error to occur. Instead, the Impact section is now hidden.

* Moving an activity under a different parent no longer causes an error to occur when the user does not have permission to access the new parent's ancestor activities.

* Opening the Budget tab section on an activity no longer causes an error to occur when the user does not have permission to access the activity's parent or ancestor activities.

## Financial Management

### Investments

* In the Activity Funding Panel, the contents of the last column is no longer cut off when the last column is a calculated column.

* Selecting the Show Unallocated Amounts option in the Filter by Activity Funding menu no longer causes an error to occur when a line item has a column value but no allocation amounts.

## Work

### Home

* Opening the board settings for certain boards in the Home module no longer displays an error message, but instead displays the Update board dialog as expected.



# Deprecations and discontinuations: 2025.7

From time to time, we must remove functionality from the Uptempo platform for various reasons. To give users time to prepare and adjust, we communicate functionality removals in two stages:

1. Deprecation: Deprecated functionality is still available in the Uptempo platform as of the current release, but is planned to be removed with a future release. Deprecated functionality usually does not receive any further updates, and only remains available to already existing users.

2. Discontinuation: Discontinued functionality has been removed from the Uptempo platform as of the current release, and is no longer available.

If functionality that you use is announced as deprecated on this page, we recommend that you begin preparing for its removal as soon as possible to ensure a smooth transition.

## Deprecations

With this release, the following functionality has been deprecated:

### End of support for Chinese, Japanese, Korean, Norwegian, Russian as Uptempo Standard Languages

As of the 2025.7 release, we are announcing the deprecation of support for the following user interface (UI) languages:

* Chinese

* Japanese

* Korean

* Norwegian

* Russian

These languages were previously included as part of the Uptempo Standard Languages, which means that Uptempo regularly provided and maintained translations of product UI text as part of ongoing releases.

Following this deprecation notice, we plan to discontinue the affected languages with the 2025.8 release. We will publish a separate discontinuation notice at that time.

Note: Note

You can manage UI languages in Uptempo under Administration > Overview > Look & Feel > UI Languages. These UI language settings apply to Uptempo Work modules, but not to Uptempo Campaign Management or Financial Management.

#### What’s changing

Support for the affected languages will officially end with the 2025.8 release on August 27, 2025. At that time:

* If you have one or more of these languages enabled in your Uptempo instance the affected languages will be automatically converted to custom languages. This means:

* These languages will be moved from the Uptempo Standard Languages section to the Your Custom Languages section.

* The moved languages will retain all translations provided by Uptempo as of the 2025.7 release, but will no longer receive updates from Uptempo.

* You can continue using and extending these translations yourself, just like any other custom language.

* If an affected language is not enabled in your Uptempo instance, it will be removed entirely, and not migrated to the Your Custom Languages section. * The exception to this is if an affected language is not enabled, but has been modified using the Customize function. In this case, the language will be treated as if it were enabled, and will be automatically moved to the Your Custom Languages section.

* New customers whose Uptempo instance is activated after July 30, 2025 will not see the discontinued languages in either section by default.

#### Need access to past translations?

If you would like to use an existing Uptempo-created translation after the discontinuation, Uptempo will provide the translation data source file upon request (with all Uptempo-provided translations as of the 2025.7 release). You can upload this file to create a new custom language in your instance.

If you have any questions or would like to request a translation file, please contact your Uptempo customer success manager, or reach out to our support team.

## Discontinuations

With this release, the following functionality has been discontinued:

None this month.



# What's new: 2025.6

Discover all the new Uptempo functionality introduced with the 2025.6 release, available from June 25, 2025.

* For details about bugs that have been fixed this month, see the [bug fixes page](bug-fixes-2025-6.html).

Note: Note

Features marked with the tag Early Access are not activated by default, but can be turned on in your Uptempo instance on request.

## Campaign Management: Activity Configuration

### List action for activity access controls
