With the new List action, administrators can now create access control statements that allow users to see an activity's name — but not any other information about that activity. You can use this type of statement to help users understand the structure of the activity hierarchy, even if they do not have full access to all activities within it. For example, a user might have access to the children of an activity, but not the parent activity itself: in this case, List access would allow that user to see that the parent activity's name is "Q4 2025 Events", which provides context to the child activities that they do have access to.

→ [Learn more about configuring access controls for activities](activity-access-control-config-overview.html)

## Campaign Management: Activities

### Save Activity Hierarchy Display Settings as Views

When you use display settings (such as filters and groupings) to customize which activities are shown in the Activity Hierarchy, you can now save these settings as views. After you save a view, you can select it from the new Views menu in the Timeline and Summary to apply the same display settings with just one click. Any user with access to the Activities section can create their own personal views that aren't visible to other users, while administrators can additionally create views that can be used by all users in their organization.

![Saved views example](images/SavedViewsExample.png)
→ [Learn more about creating and using saved views](save-activity-hierarchy-display-settings-as-views.html)

### Inherited Attribute Values in Activity Details

For any activity, you can now see exactly which attributes it has inherited from activities above it in the hierarchy, and what their values are. In Uptempo, activities have both their own attributes, and attributes that they "inherit" from their ancestor activities (parents, grandparents, etc.).

Previously, inherited attributes were not visible while viewing the details of an activity. Now, whenever you view any activity, the activity's Details section lists each ancestor activity from which attributes are being inherited: expand any listed ancestor activity to see the attributes inherited from it, along with their values. The ancestor activity names, and the inherited attributes and their values, still respect all applicable access controls, so users will only see the details that they have permission to view.

![Inherited attributes](images/InheritedAttributes.png)

### Consistent Number Formatting Configuration

Just like Uptempo Financial Management, Uptempo Campaign Management now also applies the number format preference set in each user account. When the Number format setting is configured on the Settings tab of a user account (under Administration > User List), the selected country-specific number format will now also apply to numbers displayed in the Activities section, just as it does in the Investments and Budget sections. This ensures that the way numbers are displayed matches users' expectations, which is particularly important for organizations with global teams: for example, a user in the US would see `12,345.67`, while a user in Germany would see this number as `12.345,67`.

→ [Learn more about configuring user account settings](https://knowledge.uptempo.io/doc/BM_help_en_100100000001/en-us/Content/Administration/Topics/Benutzerkonto erstellen.htm#Configur)

### Improved Expand/Collapse for Activities With Children

In the Timeline, we have improved the user experience of expanding an activity to view its child activities (and collapsing it to hide them): the size of the clickable area for the ![Expand](images/chevron-right.svg) Expand and ![Collapse](images/chevron-up.svg) Collapse arrows is now significantly larger, which makes them easier to click, and makes frustrating misclicks much less likely.

### Improved "Is Between" Date Range Filter

We've made the behavior of the is between operator for the In-market Dates filter more intuitive. This filter allows you to display activities with in-market dates that fall within a specified date range.

Previously, the is between operator for this filter would only display an activity if both its start and end dates fell within the specified filter date range. In response to feedback, we've adjusted this behavior to better align it with user expectations: now, as long as any date within an activity's in-market date range falls inside the is between filter date range, the activity will be displayed.

For example, assume you set a filter for In-market Dates | is between | June 1 to July 31. Previously, an activity with in-market dates of July 1 to August 1 would not be displayed with this filter, because August 1 is after the filter's end date. Under the new behavior, the activity is shown with this filter, because at least part of the activity's in-market dates (July 1 to July 31) falls within the filter's start and end dates.

## Financial Management: Investments

### Automatically Distribute Funds Allocations Between Connected Activities

You can now configure specific Uptempo Financial Management columns to automatically distribute funds between connected activities.

With the new Auto Distribution feature, you can eliminate the need to do manual data entry for allocations, in particular for funds amounts imported into Uptempo from integrations (such as Committed and Actual spend amounts). When Auto Distribution is configured for a column, the system will automatically allocate the line item amount in that column to connected activities in the same proportions as a specified reference column.

For example, imagine you have a line item that is connected to two activities, and you had previously allocated its funds in the "Q3 Expected Spend" column so that 70% went to one activity, and 30% to the other activity. If you set the "Q3 Expected Spend" column as a reference for the "Q3 Actual Spend" column, any line item amount you enter into the "Q3 Actual Spend" column will also be automatically split 70/30 between the connected activities.

![Auto distribution percentages](images/AutoDistributionPercentages.png)
* In conjunction with Auto Distribution, we've also added a UX enhancement to the Activity Funding Panel: now, if a Date Range is set for a Financial Management column, cells where the column's date range overlaps with a connected activity's in-market dates are highlighted with a colored background to show at a glance where funds need to be allocated. ![Allocation date range highlighting](images/AllocationDateRangeHighlighting.png)

→ [Learn more about setting up and using Auto-Distribution for Financial Management columns](https://knowledge.uptempo.io/doc/BM_Uptempo_en_109200000000/Content/02%20Planung/Doku/05-3-6-2%20AutoDistribution.htm)

### Calculated Columns in Activity Funding Panel

The Activity Funding Panel now displays calculated columns. Previously, if a Financial Management View contained calculated columns, these columns weren't visible when allocating funds from investments to connected activities. Now, if you select a View in the Activity Funding Panel that includes calculated columns, those columns are shown in the panel. Just like elsewhere in Uptempo Financial Management, calculated columns in the Activity Funding Panel are marked with a ![Calculated](images/calculator-1.svg) Calculated icon by their column name, and their contents can't be edited.

![Calculated columns funding panel](images/CalculatedColumnsFundingPanel.png)
→  [Learn more about using the Activity Funding Panel](https://knowledge.uptempo.io/doc/BM_Uptempo_en_109200000000/Content/02 Planung/Doku/05-3-6 ConnectActivitiesInvest.htm#Manually)

### Keep Existing Activity Connections When Moving Investments

For investments that are connected to activities, moving an investment (sub-category or line item) under a different parent investment that has incompatible funding no longer disconnects the moved investment's connected activity by default.

This enhancement allows you to reorganize your activity-connected investments without the risk of unintentionally removing funding from connected activities. Now, whenever you move an investment in this situation, you will be asked to choose if you want to keep the connection on the moved investment and adjust the new parent investment's activity connections to be compatible. And, as before, you can also still choose to disconnect the moved investment's connected activity and reconfigure its activity connections later.



# Bug fixes: 2025.6

To ensure that our users can benefit from bug fixes as soon as possible, we release bug fix updates to the Uptempo platform frequently.

The following bug fixes have been released over the past four weeks, between May 28 and June 20, 2025:

## API

### Documentation

* In the API documentation, the navigation sidebar is no longer cut off, and instead scrolls to display all items.

## Campaign Management

### Activities

* When a user creates a new activity and also has permission to create a child activity under the newly created activity, they no longer need to refresh the page before the option to create a new child activity appears.

* When a quick search is applied to the Activity Hierarchy, the sort order of the displayed activities is no longer "random", and is instead always alphabetical.

* When grouping by any attribute in the Summary display mode, the Planned ROI column no longer fails to sum values (for example, in the Grand total row.)

* On activities with chained attribute dependencies, when you make a change to a dependent attribute and then, before saving that change, make another change to a non-dependent attribute that saves automatically, clicking Cancel on the dependent attribute change will no longer revert the non-dependent attribute to its previous value.

* The Outbound Action dialog is no longer significantly smaller in Campaign Management than its counterpart in Financial Management.

* When you set a filter in the Activity Hierarchy, the filter no longer remains active after you edit the URL to remove the filter's parameter.

* After duplicating an activity, trying to change Estimated Costs quarters on one of the activities no longer causes an error.

* When setting a date range for Estimated Costs, setting an end date that is earlier than the start date no longer causes an error. Instead, the system will automatically switch the two dates in this situation.

* When changing start and end dates on Estimated Costs, the changes no longer fail to save in some cases.

* In the Budget Picker (for connecting investments to an activity), the Open (arrow) button on the investment selection menu is no longer unresponsive, and clicking it now opens the menu as expected.

## Financial Management

### Audit Trail

* When you enter a partial username into the search field, the Audit Trail no longer fails to return entries that match the partial username.

### Investments

* When you use the search field to filter the activities shown in the Activity Picker (for connecting activities to an investment), selecting an activity to connect no longer causes the filter to be reset.

* In the Filter by Activity Funding menu, the Open (arrow) button is no longer unresponsive, and clicking it now opens the menu as expected.

* When an investment Sub-Category is a child of a Category or Sub-Category that is linked to an activity, it is no longer displayed without an icon that indicates the link.

* When there are approved transfers in the hierarchy, the system now displays a message when a user adds a new currency to the hierarchy to warn users that doing so may cause unexpected conversion results for the approved transfers.



# What's new: 2025.5

Discover all the new Uptempo functionality introduced with the 2025.5 release, available from May 28, 2025.

* For details about bugs that have been fixed this month, see the [bug fixes page](bug-fixes-2025-5.html).

Note: Note

Features marked with the tag Early Access are not activated by default, but can be turned on in your Uptempo instance on request.

## Campaign Management: API

### More New API Endpoints for Multi-Activity Funding APIs

To give developers more flexibility in integrating external systems with Uptempo's Multi-Activity Funding feature, we have created more new API endpoints to interact with this feature. These new capabilities unlock additional ways to use the API to interact with Uptempo's activity funding mechanisms:

* You can now modify allocations of line item funds amounts (including the unallocated amount) to connected activities, with support for original and converted currencies, as well as the ability to set negative unallocated amounts.

* While modifying allocation amounts, you can now also connect new activities with the same API request.

[Learn more about how to use the Multi-Activity Funding API endpoints](https://api-na.allocadia.com/v1/docs/secured/index.xml#activity-investments)

## Financial Management: Investments

### Enhanced Filtering Capabilities for Budget Allocation Tracking

We have added more filtering options to the investment hierarchy table to give you better insight into activity funding and funds allocation. Previously, you could filter the table to see only investments that are connected to activities. With the new and expanded Filter by Activity Funding options, you can now additionally see the specific amounts allocated to connected activities. And the opposite view is now also available, allowing you to see investment amounts not yet allocated to any activity. These new filter options provide a more complete view of how your investments support activities, helping you to manage budgets and make informed decisions.

![Filter by activity funding updated](images/FilterByActivityFundingUpdated.png)
→ [Learn more about filtering investments by activity funding](https://knowledge.uptempo.io/doc/BM_Uptempo_en_109200000000/Content/03 Management/06-5a FilterActivityFunding.htm)

### Calculated Columns in Activity Funding Panel

The Activity Funding Panel now displays calculated columns. Previously, if a Financial Management View contained calculated columns, these columns weren't visible when allocating funds from investments to connected activities. Now, if you select a View in the Activity Funding Panel that includes calculated columns, those columns are shown in the panel. Just like elsewhere in Uptempo Financial Management, calculated columns in the Activity Funding Panel are marked with a ![Calculated](images/calculator-1.svg) Calculated icon by their column name, and their contents can't be edited.

![Calculated columns funding panel](images/CalculatedColumnsFundingPanel.png)
→  [Learn more about using the Activity Funding Panel](https://knowledge.uptempo.io/doc/BM_Uptempo_en_109200000000/Content/02 Planung/Doku/05-3-6 ConnectActivitiesInvest.htm#Manually)



# Bug fixes: 2025.5

To ensure that our users can benefit from bug fixes as soon as possible, we release bug fix updates to the Uptempo platform frequently.

The following bug fixes have been released over the past four weeks, between April 28 and May 23, 2025:

## Administration

### User List

* When new users are invited to Uptempo Financial Management, they no longer fail to appear in the User List in some cases.

### Permissions

* In the Planner subsection of the Permissions page, the Users count for each permission no longer incorrectly shows "0" when there are users assigned to the permission.

## API

### Authentication

* In the API documentation, the example request header for authentication no longer incorrectly says "token" instead of "Bearer" (which would prevent the request from working if the example request was used as provided).

## Campaign Management

### Activities

* After you create a new activity, the Timeline display mode now ensures that the newly created activity is visible by automatically scrolling to it (and expanding parent activities above it if needed).

* When creating a new activity using the Add Under button, the activity setup assistant no longer exhibits erroneous behaviour in some cases (such as failing to load, or displaying blank dropdown menus).

* When you are configuring a multi-select attribute where percentage allocations are enabled, highlighting the percentage value for a selected list option no longer causes that list option to become deselected.

* When no activity types have been defined in an Uptempo instance yet, the Create Activity button is no longer hidden in the Activities section. Instead, the button is now visible but deactivated, and displays a tooltip to more clearly explain that creating activities is not possible until at least one activity type has been set up.

* Overriding roll-up values on the Details > Impact tab no longer causes the activity's In-Market Dates to be changed inadvertently.

### Activity Configuration

* For access control statements that contain a condition which specifies activity type group IDs, opening the Conditions info box for the statement no longer incorrectly displays the names of activity types that happen to have the same IDs, but instead correctly displays the names of the specified type groups.

* For access control statements that contain an IS ONE OF condition and a large number of specified values, opening the Conditions info box for the statement no longer causes the long list of condition values to be cut off.

* When you try to delete an activity type that still has associated activities, activity rules, or outbound actions, the system now displays a more helpful error message to explain why you can't delete the activity type.

* The Set your default average deal size field on the Measures > Impact Modeller configuration page now uses the currency set as the Primary Plan Currency (on the Activities > Currencies page), instead of always displaying USD.

## Financial Management

### Investments

* The Activity Funding Panel no longer displays duplicate allocation amounts.

* When a line item's amount is entered in a local currency and converted, trying to allocate the full amount of the line item in the Activity Funding Panel no longer causes an error when trying to save the allocations.

* The icons used to expand UI items are no longer inconsistent.

* The Select activity to connect to... dialog no longer fails to display an activity's type under the Type column.

* Moving an investment under a different parent category or sub-category no longer causes the color-coding and icons that symbolize activity connections to disappear from the investments table.

* Creating a calculated column when there are already 15 columns or more in the investments table no longer causes an error that prevents the calculated column from being edited or deleted.

## Work

### All Modules

* When you stay on the same page for a long time (more than 30 minutes), reloading the page or navigating to a different page no longer causes the navigation sidebar menu to disappear.



# What's new: 2025.4

Discover all the new Uptempo functionality introduced with the 2025.4 release, available from April 30, 2025.

* For details about bugs that have been fixed this month, see the [bug fixes page](bug-fixes-2025-3.html).

* For notifications about functionality that is planned to be retired, see the [deprecations and discontinuations page](deprecations-and-discontinuations-2025-4.html).

Note: Note

Features marked with the tag Early Access are not activated by default, but can be turned on in your Uptempo instance on request.

## Campaign Management: Activities

### Expanded Access Controls for Activities

To give administrators more control over their users' access to the activity hierarchy, we've introduced several major new capabilities to the Uptempo Campaign Management access controls:

* Activity Conditions: With Activity Conditions, you can now define access to activities with much greater granularity. Instead of only being able to grant full access to all activities of a given activity type or type group, you can now set conditions that pinpoint specific activities based on their attributes. For example, you can use conditions to give users access only to activities relevant to their geographic region or department. By combining multiple conditions, you can get as granular as needed to tailor access levels to your organizations exact needs.

* View Action: Previously, access controls only allowed you to grant full access to activities — to see an activity, a user also had to have the ability to create or edit that activity. Using the new View action, you can now easily set up read-only access for users who need to be able to see an activity's details, but shouldn't have the ability to modify the activity.

* Set Activity As Child Of Action: To help maintain the integrity of complex activity hierarchies, the new Set Activity As Child Of action allows you to define exactly where in the hierarchy users can place activities. This means that users can only select hierarchy locations (parent activities) that have been predefined as valid whenever they create or move an activity, ensuring that your business logic is consistently enforced.

[Learn more about configuring access controls for activities](activity-access-control-config-overview.html)

### Create Attribute Dependency Chains

When you configure attributes for your activity types, you can now connect multiple (non-required) attribute dependencies together to create multi-level dependency chains.

Previously, you could set up pairs of attributes where one was a "controlling" attribute for a second "dependent" attribute, for example a Country attribute might be set to control the values for a dependent State attribute — however, an attribute set as a dependent could not itself be set as the controlling attribute for a separate dependent attribute. That restriction has now been removed, which means that you can chain dependencies by setting any attribute as both a controlling and dependent attribute at the same time. For example, a State attribute could be the dependent of a Country attribute, and the controlling attribute for a City attribute, creating a Country-City-State dependency chain.

[Learn more about creating attribute dependencies](attribute-dependencies.html)

### Keep Existing Funding When Moving Activities

For activities that are connected to investments, moving an activity under a different parent activity that has incompatible funding no longer disconnects the moved activity's connected investment by default. This enhancement allows you to reorganize your investment-connected activities without the risk of unintentionally removing their funding.

Now, whenever you move an activity in this situation, you will be asked to choose if you want to keep the connection on the moved activity and adjust the new parent activity's investment connections to be compatible. And, as before, you can also still choose to disconnect the moved activity's connected investment and reconfigure its funding afterward.

### Track and Compare Actual Performance Metrics for Activities

Track your campaign performance with intuitive KPI comparisons and enhanced visuals. You can now see how Actual results stack up versus Planned through every level of your campaign hierarchy. Choose monthly or quarterly views for granular insights, and quickly spot positive trends with our new improvement direction indicators.

[Learn more about how to track and compare actual performance metrics for activities](track-and-analyze-kpis.html)

### Persistent Activity Expanded/Collapsed State

Uptempo now remembers whether you expanded or collapsed a parent activity in the Activity column of the Timeline display mode. Whenever you come back to the Timeline display mode (or refresh the page) in the same browser, all activities will be displayed as expanded or collapsed just as you had left them the last time. Your settings are saved in the browser's local storage, so they'll be remembered even if you close the tab or browser window.

## Campaign Management: API

### Read/Update API for Actual KPIs

This feature allows you to automatically update KPI values through integrations by connecting with external data sources. It allows daily data capture while displaying monthly aggregates in the interface for better business usability. Visual indicators show when values are updated via API.

### New API Endpoints for Multi-Activity Funding

We've added features to the Campaign Management API that you can use to perform actions related to funds allocations on activity-investment connections. For example, you can now use the API to programmatically export all funds allocation details from a line item, including the total line item value and allocation amounts, in both the original and converted currencies (if applicable).

[Learn more about how to use the Multi-Activity Funding API endpoints](https://api-na.allocadia.com/v1/docs/secured/index.xml#activity-investments)

## Work: Jobs and Media Pool

### Upload Images Using Copy & Paste

You can now paste images directly from your clipboard into the Asset Selector in Job Manager or the Upload window in Media Pool. This makes uploading images easier and more intuitive, and eliminates the need to save image files before uploading them: just copy an image using `Ctrl + C` or `⌘ + C` from any source, open the upload dialog, and paste the image with `Ctrl + V` or `⌘ + V` to upload it.

[Learn more about adding content in the Asset Selector of a job datasheet](https://knowledge.uptempo.io/doc/BM_help_en_100700000002/en-us/Content/Benutzer/03 Edit Job/Assets hinzufügen.htm)

[Learn more about uploading assets for Media Pool](https://knowledge.uptempo.io/doc/BM_help_en_100200000002/en-us/Content/Benutzer/01 Objekt-Tasks/01 Anlegen/Upload.htm)



# Bug fixes: 2025.4

To ensure that our users can benefit from bug fixes as soon as possible, we release bug fix updates to the Uptempo platform frequently.

The following bug fixes have been released over the past four weeks, between March 24 and April 25, 2025:

## Administration

### User List

* The user management system no longer creates a duplicate user if a user is created with the same email as an existing user, but different capitalization (for example user.name@email.com and USER.NAME@email.com).

## Analytics

### Insights

* The list of dashboards no longer displays a scrollbar when there are more than four dashboards to display.

## Campaign Management

### API

* The JSON generated by the API in binary file output is no longer broken.

* For any GET endpoint that returns the details of a line item, the property hasManuallyAllocatedInvestment no longer returns the value null when no value is set, and instead returns the more accurate value false.

### Activities

* When you open the details panel for any activity in the Timeline display mode, the panel no longer briefly displays a message that the activity is no longer available while the activity's details are loading.

* In the Timeline display mode, the Zoom Out button is now deactivated when the maximum zoom level is reached, to more clearly indicate when there are no additional zoom levels available.

* The activity details panel no longer displays the error message that the selected parent is not valid under the Parent field.

* In the activity details panel, it is no longer possible to enter dates more than 5 years before or after the current date in the date fields on the KPIs and Estimated Costs tabs (previously, doing so would cause an error).

* When creating an activity, the Next button is no longer incorrectly deactivated for certain combinations of values in the Activity Type and Parent fields.

* When creating an activity, it is no longer possible to select an activity type that does not have any activity rules defined (previously, doing so would cause an erroneous error message to be shown).

* When creating an activity, it is no longer possible to select an activity type that has been disabled (previously, doing so would cause activity creation to fail).

* When creating an activity, it is no longer possible to enter an activity name that is longer than the system limit of 128 characters (previously, doing so would cause activity creation to fail).

* When creating an activity, the system no longer incorrectly displays an error message on the Budget step when you connect an investment item and then disconnect it again.

* After creating an activity, the success notification message no longer immediately disappears, and is instead displayed long enough to be read.

* When you open the details panel for an activity that has already been deleted (for example deleted by another user before you had refreshed the page), the system no longer displays an empty details panel, and instead displays an error message.

### Activity Configuration

* When you attempt to delete an activity rule that has already been deleted (for example deleted by another user before you had refreshed the page), the system no longer displays an inaccurate error message.

* The error message that is displayed when you attempt to delete an activity rule that is still in use has been improved to be clearer and more informative.

* When you set a custom label for Estimated Costs, the wording "estimated costs" is no longer used in the success notification after you make changes to an activity's estimated costs, and the specified custom label is used instead.

## Financial Management

### Investments

* In the Activity Funding Panel, you no longer need to press the Delete or Backspace buttons twice after highlighting a column value to remove it.

* In the Activity Funding Panel, the Activity column is no longer displayed with an extremely wide width when the column contains an activity with a very long name.



# Deprecations and discontinuations: 2025.4

From time to time, we must remove functionality from the Uptempo platform for various reasons. To give users time to prepare and adjust, we communicate functionality removals in two stages:

1. Deprecation: Deprecated functionality is still available in the Uptempo platform as of the current release, but is planned to be removed with a future release. Deprecated functionality usually does not receive any further updates, and only remains available to already existing users.

2. Discontinuation: Discontinued functionality has been removed from the Uptempo platform as of the current release, and is no longer available.

If functionality that you use is announced as deprecated on this page, we recommend that you begin preparing for its removal as soon as possible to ensure a smooth transition.

## Deprecations

With this release, the following functionality has been deprecated:

### Basic Authentication for BI Export API

As of the 2025.4 release, we are deprecating basic authentication as a method for accessing the Uptempo/Allocadia [BI Export API](https://knowledge.uptempo.io/doc/BM_Uptempo_en_109200000000/Content/06 Administration/10 APITechDocu/A9 BI Export API.htm). This change affects users of all supported versions of the BI Export API.

Previously, basic authentication was the only supported authentication method for legacy APIs, including the BI Export API. To enhance security for users of these APIs, we have incorporated the BI Export API endpoints into the Allocadia API, which uses bearer token authentication. As a result, you can now interact with the BI Export API endpoints via the Allocadia API, allowing you to access the same capabilities in a more secure way. For more information about the Allocadia API, including how to authenticate, see our [API documentation](https://api-na.allocadia.com/v1/docs/secured/index.xml).

Because the Allocadia API now supports all the functions of the legacy BI Export API, accessing the BI Export API using basic authentication is now deprecated, and will be discontinued in the future. As with all Uptempo product deprecations, the basic authentication method will remain available until further notice, and no discontinuation date has been set at this time. However, if you currently use the BI Export API, we strongly encourage you to migrate to the Allocadia API at your earliest convenience to ensure a smooth transition, and to benefit from modern, secure authentication for your requests.

## Discontinuations

With this release, the following functionality has been discontinued:

### Enable Tasks and Worklogs Setting

The Enable Tasks and Worklogs setting has been removed from Other Settings in Datasheet Engine Administration. This setting was obsolete, as the associated product functionality had been discontinued previously.

This change simplifies the interface by removing an unused setting. No action is required from you, and this removal won't affect any existing configurations.



# What's new: 2025.3

Discover all the new Uptempo functionality introduced with the 2025.3 release, available from March 26, 2025.

* For details about bugs that have been fixed this month, see the [bug fixes page](bug-fixes-2025-3.html).

Note: Note

Features marked with the tag Early Access are not activated by default, but can be turned on in your Uptempo instance on request.

## Campaign Management: Activities

### Compare Activities by Actual ROI

The Activities Summary display mode now features an Actual ROI column displaying calculated return percentages with color-coded formatting (green for positive, red for negative) to rapidly identify high-performing investments versus underperforming activities. This enables you to make data-driven decisions about resource allocation based on demonstrated financial performance.

![Actual roi](images/ActualROI.png)
[Learn more about comparing activities by Actual ROI](customize-summary-table.html)

### Activity Name Updates via API

As a developer you can now programmatically update activity names through our APIs. This enhancement ensures consistent activity hierarchies when names change in Uptempo or in connected applications. Previously, when an activity name changed in an integrated system, administrators had to manually update these names in our platform. Now, these updates synchronize automatically, saving time and ensuring naming consistency.

### Persistent Activity Column Width

When you change the width of the Activity column in the Timeline display mode, the custom width you set is now saved in the browser's local storage. This means that the Activity column will now stay the same width every time you refresh or reopen the Timeline display mode in the same browser — even if you closed the tab or browser window.

### Track and Compare Actual Performance Metrics for Activities

Track your campaign performance with intuitive KPI comparisons and enhanced visuals. You can now see how Actual results stack up versus Planned through every level of your campaign hierarchy. Choose monthly or quarterly views for granular insights, and quickly spot positive trends with our new improvement direction indicators.

[Learn more about how to track and compare actual performance metrics for activities](track-and-analyze-kpis.html)

## Campaign Management:  API

### Activity Impact Planned API

The new API endpoint provides key metrics for impact-based activities, providing detailed quantity and revenue projections across multiple funnel stages. With this dedicated feature, enterprise systems can easily retrieve planned impact values using a simple GET request, thus enabling seamless data integration and performance analysis.

### Read/Update API for Actual KPIs

This feature allows you to automatically update KPI values through integrations by connecting with external data sources. It allows daily data capture while displaying monthly aggregates in the interface for better business usability. Visual indicators show when values are updated via API.

## Financial Management: Investments

### Enhanced Filtering Capabilities for Budget Allocation Tracking

We have added more filtering options to the investment hierarchy table to give you better insight into activity funding and funds allocation. Previously, you could filter the table to see only investments that are connected to activities. With the new and expanded Filter by Activity Funding options, you can now additionally see the specific amounts allocated to connected activities. And the opposite view is now also available, allowing you to see investment amounts not yet allocated to any activity. These new filter options provide a more complete view of how your investments support activities, helping you to manage budgets and make informed decisions.

![Filter by activity funding](images/FilterByActivityFunding.png)
[Learn more about how to filter investments by activity funding](https://knowledge.uptempo.io/doc/BM_Uptempo_en_109200000000/Content/03 Management/06-5a FilterActivityFunding.htm)



# Bug fixes: 2025.3

To ensure that our users can benefit from bug fixes as soon as possible, we release bug fix updates to the Uptempo platform frequently.

The following bug fixes have been released over the past four weeks, between February 24 and March 21, 2025:

## Administration

### All

* Throughout the Administration section, UI labels will no longer show placeholder text in some cases, and will instead correctly show the specified label text.

### SSO Groups

* If an external SSO system is used to provision users in Uptempo, the Uptempo system-default SSO group will no longer be automatically assigned to new users who are not recognized by the external SSO system when this group is deactivated (when the Use as default setting is turned off for this group).

### User List

* Storage Groups (VDBs) that were missing after upgrading to BrandMaker 8.0/Uptempo Work are once again visible, and can be assigned to users again.

## Campaign Management

### Activities

* When you open the Create Activity setup assistant, the footer in the dialog is now displayed at the correct width, and the Next button is no longer cut off.

* In the Create Activity setup assistant, entering a value into the Planned Inquiries field (under Impact > Planned) and then deleting it no longer causes an error message to be displayed.

* In the Create Activity setup assistant, when in-market dates are set as required, clicking the heading of the second step (for example Details) instead of the Next button no longer causes the setup assistant to crash.

### Activity Configuration

* When you create an activity and select an Activity Type that does not require a parent activity, the Next button is no longer deactivated until the Activity Type has been selected a second time.

## Financial Management

### Investments

* When you connect an activity to an investment, entering a search term that matches a very large number of activities no longer causes an error.

* When you are in the Investments section, hovering on any other section in the navigation sidebar no longer displays duplicated tooltips for the name of the hovered section.

* If you leave a tab with a Financial Management module page open but inactive for some time, returning to the tab later no longer causes a white page with the text "null" to be displayed. Instead, the system now correctly allows you to resume using the page (if your session is still active), or redirects you to the login page (if your session has expired).

## Other Fixes

### API

* Using ActivityCollection.getAll to retrieve all activities no longer causes the connection to the API to drop with a timeout error in cases where the response takes longer than usual.



# What's new: 2025.2

Discover all the new Uptempo functionality introduced with the 2025.2 release, available from February 26, 2025.

* For details about bugs that have been fixed this month, see the [bug fixes page](bug-fixes-2025-2.html).

* For notifications about functionality that is planned to be retired, see the [deprecations and discontinuations page](deprecations-and-discontinuations-2025-2.html).

Note: Note

Features marked with the tag Early Access are not activated by default, but can be turned on in your Uptempo instance on request.

## Uptempo Platform: Administration

### New Status attribute for user accounts

With the new Status attribute, shown by default on the User List, administrators can now view the current status of all user accounts in their Uptempo instance at a glance. As an admin, you can quickly see if a user account is currently active; if it has not yet been activated or has been manually deactivated; or if it has been temporarily locked out due to failed login attempts. You can also change user statuses right from the User List, making it simple to manage your user accounts for security and licensing purposes.

![User list status](images/UserListStatus.png)
[Learn more about managing user accounts](https://knowledge.uptempo.io/doc/BM_help_en_100100000001/en-us/Content/Administration/Topics/Benutzerkonto bearbeiten.htm)

## Campaign Management: Activities

### Turn the Estimated Costs feature on or off for individual activity types

Previously, the Estimated Costs feature was activated by default for all activity types, and could not be hidden from activity types where it was not applicable. Now, administrators can turn Estimated Costs on or off for specific activity types: if you turn this feature off for an activity type, the Estimated Costs tab is not shown on the details panel of any activity of that type.

Note: Note

If you want to deactivate the Estimated Costs feature for an activity type that already has activities Estimated Costs values set, you must first remove all these values from the activities.

![Turn off estimated costs](images/TurnOffEstimatedCosts.png)
[Learn more about configuring activity types](create-and-configure-activity-types.html)

### Support for negative amounts in Estimated Costs

The Estimated Costs feature now supports entering amounts starting with a minus (for example `-4000`) to indicate when a cost estimate should be reduced by some amount. This is useful in cases where a marketing activity is being subsidized by external funding, such as a partner sponsorship or grant. Negative amounts are supported for both manual inputs and via the API, and Estimated Costs roll-ups accurately take negative amounts into account by subtracting them from the total.

![Estimated negative](images/EstimatedNegative.png)
[Learn more about estimating activity costs](track-and-analyze-budgets.html)

### Terminology update: Data Granularity → Time Granularity

For consistency, we have renamed the settings Input Format (in Activity Configuration > Estimated Costs) and Data Granularity (in details panel > Impact > Actuals). Both settings are now called Time Granularity to better reflect that these settings control the level of detail used to capture and display data with regard to timeframe (for example monthly, quarterly, etc.).

## Financial Management: API

### Support for more Spend data categories

Uptempo Financial Management currently supports four separate data categories (Planned, Committed, Actual, and Expected), but previously, only data related to the Planned data category was available via the API. Now, you can use the API to pull data related to all the Spend data categories, for example for integration with other systems.

## Work: Review Manager

### Invite multiple users to reviews in one step

To save time when setting up reviews, you can now invite multiple users to a review at once. The redesigned Add Participants dialog now supports adding more than one user to a review, so you no longer need to add them one-by-one. You can even choose to add a user group to a review, which adds all the group's members to the review with a single click. When you add a user group to a review, its members are listed separately as review participants, so you can still assign roles to each participant individually.

![Invite group to review](images/InviteGroupToReview.png)
[Learn more about inviting participants to reviews](https://knowledge.uptempo.io/doc/BM_help_en_101100000002/en-us/Content/Benutzer/01 Objekt-Tasks/Teilnehmer einladen.htm)

### Search by job name

The search function in the Review Manager widget can now find reviews based on the name of the connected job (from Job Manager). To find reviews using the context of their connected job, type the job name into the search field, and the list of reviews is filtered to display only reviews where the connected job name matches your search term.

[Learn more about the Review Manager Start Page](https://knowledge.uptempo.io/doc/BM_help_en_101100000002/en-us/Content/Benutzer/02 Interface/Startseite.htm)

## Work: Marketing Planner

### Enhanced Sort and Filter dialog

The Sort and Filter dialog now features a cleaner, more intuitive interface with logically grouped sections and helpful descriptions for each filtering option. When you open the dialog, you'll find improved tooltips and organized categories that guide you through filtering activities by type, structure, or timing – making it simpler to find exactly the data you need.

To use the new features, click Sort and Filter in the toolbar and explore the streamlined filtering options. You'll notice each section now includes clear descriptions and tooltips that explain their purpose, such as filtering by activity details or organizing by structural relationships.

![Sort and filter feb25](images/SortAndFilterFeb25.png)
[Learn more about using Sort and Filter](https://knowledge.uptempo.io//doc/BM_help_en_100300000002/en-us/Content/03 Management/14-0 Filtern Sortieren.htm)



# Bug fixes: 2025.2

To ensure that our users can benefit from bug fixes as soon as possible, we release bug fix updates to the Uptempo product frequently.

The following bug fixes have been released over the past four weeks, between January 27 and February 21, 2025:

## Campaign Management

### Activities

* The validation for the total sum of percentage allocations on multi-select attributes is no longer inaccurate.

* In cases where a dependent attribute and its controlling attribute both use the multi-select attribute type, the lists of options for both attributes no longer sometimes appear in the wrong order.

* When you start to move an activity to a different part of the hierarchy by selecting a new parent (in the Parent list of the activity's Details section), but then cancel the move, the new parent activity no longer remains selected in the Parent list on the Details section. Instead, it correctly rolls back to the previously selected parent activity.

* In the Summary display mode, the system will no longer display an error message when you select the Impact columns and then set a filter that doesn't return any activities.

* If the Quarterly Estimated Costs feature is enabled, quarterly values will no longer re-appear after you attempt to delete them. Instead, deleted values will now be removed as expected.

* The Impact Projection chart on the Details > Impact tab of an activity no longer displays an incorrect start date after you click Sync Activity.

* For Estimated Costs values that use the Quarterly time granularity setting, certain decimal values (for example 0.3) will no longer cause incorrect total sums due to rounding errors.

### Activity Configuration

* When you delete an access control policy statement, the confirmation message popover no longer appears on all visible policy statements, and is instead shown only on the policy statement being deleted.

* When you update an activity type but don't change its activity type group, the system no longer attempts to unnecessarily check if the assigned activity type group is valid. Instead, this validation now happens only in cases where the assigned activity type group has actually been changed.

* When you try to assign an activity type to an activity type group that is incompatible due to an attribute conflict, the incompatible activity type group no longer remains selected in the Activity Type Group list, but instead correctly rolls back to the previously selected group.

## Financial Management

### Budget

* The Activity Linked Item option is no longer missing from the Area list menu on the Budget section's audit trail.

### Investments

* When the investments list is filtered to show only investments that fund activities, new line items no longer fail to appear in the list after they are created.

* Deselecting the option Show $0 rows on the investments list when the option Use default filter settings is also selected will no longer cause all the currently selected filter options to be deselected.

* Selecting both the Show $0 rows and Show activity linked items options on the investments list no longer causes investments without activity links to be included in the output from the Export Current view function.

* The system will no longer incorrectly display a "view-only" icon in the Funding Panel on activities to which a user has full access, but which is set to Automatic Allocation.

* When you connect an activity from an investment's details panel, the Select an activity to connect dialog no longer misleadingly displays the message "No Data" while the list of activities is still loading. Now, the loading spinner is shown until the activities list has finished loading.

## Other Fixes

### Analytics

* Generating GoodData project files in an account where Activities and Multi-Activity Funding are enabled, but no GoodData projects are configured yet, no longer results in an error. Instead, the GoodData project files will be generated as expected.

### BI Exports

* When you generate a report for Planned Impact, the Activity Measure export file is no longer missing data for the top-of-funnel stage (Stage 1, or Planned Inquiries by default), and data for the subsequent stages is no longer misattributed to the preceding stage in the funnel.



# Deprecations and discontinuations: 2025.2

From time to time, we must remove functionality from the Uptempo platform for various reasons. To give users time to prepare and adjust, we communicate functionality removals in two stages:

1. Deprecation: Deprecated functionality is still available in the Uptempo platform as of the current release, but is planned to be removed with a future release. Deprecated functionality usually does not receive any further updates, and only remains available to already existing users.

2. Discontinuation: Discontinued functionality has been removed from the Uptempo platform as of the current release, and is no longer available.

If functionality that you use is announced as deprecated on this page, we recommend that you begin preparing for its removal as soon as possible to ensure a smooth transition.

## Deprecations

With this release, the following functionality has been deprecated:

### Microsoft Office Add-In

With the release of version 8.0, the Microsoft Office Add-In enters deprecation status. This change reflects our ongoing efforts to streamline our integration ecosystem and ensure sustainable long-term support for our Microsoft Office integrations. Customers currently using the Microsoft Office Add-In to search and include assets from Media Pool should be aware that the Add-in will not be actively maintained or supported as part of the standard product offering in version 8.0 and beyond.

#### Options for Current Users of the Add-In

We understand that the Microsoft Office Add-In for assets is an important part of some customers' workflows. To accommodate these needs, we offer the following option:

* Extended Support Program: Customers can opt for continued support and individual development of the add-in through a dedicated maintenance agreement. This program includes: * Technical support for the Add-in * Compatibility updates for version 8.0 * Ongoing maintenance and bug fixes * Custom development as needed

#### Next Steps

Customers who rely on the Microsoft Office Add-In and wish to migrate to version 8.0 should contact their Customer Success representative in order to:

* Discuss their specific use cases and requirements

* Receive a detailed assessment of continuation costs and effort

* Develop a tailored support plan

## Discontinuations

With this release, the following functionality has been discontinued:

None this month.



# What's new: 2025.1

Discover all the new Uptempo functionality introduced with the 2025.1 release, available from January 31, 2025.

Note: Note

Features marked with the tag Early Access are not activated by default, but can be turned on in your Uptempo instance on request.

## Campaign Management: Activity Configuration

### Set Estimated Costs fields as required for an activity type

We have added the option to set Estimated Costs fields as required for any activity type. You can enable this option for an activity type if you want to require users to fill out the Estimated Costs fields whenever they create or edit an activity of that type.

[Learn more about configuring activity types](create-and-configure-activity-types.html)

## Campaign Management: Activities

### Estimated Costs roll-ups at all levels

To better support bottom-up planning, Estimated Costs now roll up all the way to the top of the activity hierarchy in both the Summary display mode, and any activity's details panel. Previously, Estimated Costs would only roll up one level: an activity displayed a roll-up total if its child activities (one level down) had Estimated Costs set, but not if the Estimated Costs were set on activities two levels down or lower. Now, activities always display an Estimated Costs roll-up total, no matter how many levels down the values are set. For example, the top-level activity will display a roll-up total even if all the Estimated Costs are set only on activities at the bottom of the hierarchy, six or seven levels down. And just like before, if an activity on a level in between has its own Estimated Costs value set, this value will override the roll-up total for that level, and will be used to calculate roll-up totals for levels above it.

[Learn more about working with Estimated Costs](track-and-analyze-budgets.html)

### Summary display mode performance improvements

We have implemented a variety of performance optimizations to ensure that the Summary display mode loads quickly and remains responsive, even if the table contains hundreds of activities.

[Learn more about Summary display mode](get-started-campaign.html)

## Financial Management: Investments

### Improved user experience when connecting activities to investments

Based on user feedback, we have implemented a set of UX/UI enhancements to improve the user experience when you connect Uptempo Campaign Management activities to Uptempo Financial Management investments. The changes include tweaks to various actions, workflows, and system behaviors, so that they are now more intuitive and easier to use. We have also revised numerous text elements (including button labels, tooltips, messages, and more) to more clearly communicate how the feature works.

[Learn more about funding activities with investments](https://knowledge.uptempo.io/doc/BM_Uptempo_en_109200000000/Content/02 Planung/Doku/05-3-6 ConnectActivitiesInvest.htm)

### Exporting investment data now supports the linked activities filter

When you use the export function to download investment data from Uptempo Financial Management, the Export Current View option will now take the linked activities filter into account. If the Show activity linked items filter option is enabled when you perform an export, the exported data will now include only investments that have links to Uptempo Campaign Management activities.

[Learn more about exporting investment data](https://knowledge.uptempo.io/doc/BM_Uptempo_en_109200000000/Content/02 Planung/Doku/05-6 ExportInvestments.htm)

## Uptempo Platform: Administration

### Automated user creation via SAML for SSO integration

Single sign-on integration now automates account creation and synchronization using identity provider data across all Uptempo modules, thus streamlining enterprise user management. The enhanced process ensures consistent permissions through group features while eliminating manual user entry requirements for Campaign Management & Financial Management users.

[Learn more about SSO/SAML as a platform administrator](https://knowledge.uptempo.io/doc/BM_Uptempo_en_105000000000/Content/01 Uptempo/Uptempo.htm?Highlight=SSO)

### Redesigned user profile page

The previous user profile page was challenging for users to efficiently manage their settings or navigate away from the profile view. To enhance usability, we have introduced a standalone profile page with a better user experience. The new design retains all existing profile information.

![User profile redesign](images/UserProfileRedesign.png)

### Improved user experience for User & General administration

We have enhanced the administrator experience by consolidating all user-related configurations into a single, intuitive interface, eliminating the previous scattered approach to user management.

[Learn more about User Administration](https://knowledge.uptempo.io/doc/BM_help_en_100100000001/en-us/Content/Administration/Topics/Benutzerverwaltung.htm)

### Unified navigation menu

Uptempo now features a unified menu for Campaign Management, Financial Management and Work, improving the user experience and facilitating movement between modules. The new navigation sidebar allows you to seamlessly switch between different Uptempo modules, and eliminates the need to switch between separate navigation menus for the Campaign Management/Financial Management modules and the Work modules.

### New support experience in Work

We have expanded our new in-app interactive support experience to Uptempo Work, to match the experience already available in Campaign Management & Financial Management. Uptempo Work users can now use the new support interface to directly search our help content, message the Support team, see news and announcements, and more — all without leaving the Uptempo product interface.

## Uptempo Platform: Central Analytics

### User List Report with export

To address a gap in version 8.0 system administration capabilities, we have reimplemented full user data export functionality in Tableau. This functionality enables comprehensive reporting of user activities and role assignments in Excel, empowering administrators to effectively monitor system usage, ensure license compliance, and make data-driven decisions about user management, while providing deeper insights than the previous export functionality offered.

[Learn more about User List Report](https://knowledge.uptempo.io/doc/BM_Uptempo_en_700100000000/Content/UserTopics/user_activity_history.htm)

### User Activity Report

User Activity Reporting now integrates with Uptempo's user identity and access management system (Keycloak). This improved solution replaces fragmented legacy BrandMaker reporting tools, and provides consistent, detailed insights into user engagement metrics including login patterns and session data, while ensuring proper time zone handling and authentication support.

[Learn more about User Activity History](https://knowledge.uptempo.io/doc/BM_Uptempo_en_700100000000/Content/UserTopics/user_activity_overview.htm)

### User Permission Report (Tableau)

To resolve data access limitations related to the transition from legacy systems to the Uptempo unified platform, we have reworked the User Permission Report into a dual-reporting system. The new system consists of two reports: one in Tableau, which contains comprehensive user and budget data (such as login dates, names, etc.); and one in GoodData, which contains data about user roles and permissions. By using these two reports in tandem, administrators can maintain complete oversight of user management and access. This release makes the Tableau report available, joining the GoodData report that was previously released in the October 2024 release.

![User permission report](images/UserPermissionReport.png)
[Learn more about User Permission Report](https://knowledge.uptempo.io/doc/BM_Uptempo_en_700100000000/Content/Home.htm)



# Release: November 2025

The November 2025 release for Hive9 is available from November 26, 2025, and adds these features to the platform:

## Accrual Requests now recoverable from the Recycle Bin

The Recycle Bin in Hive9 now also supports Accrual Requests, making it possible to undo accidental deletions.

Previously, deleting an Accrual Request (AR) was permanent. Now, whenever you delete an AR it will appear in the Recycle Bin, just like other recoverable resources. In the Recycle Bin, select the new Accrual Requests option to view deleted ARs, and click Restore to recover any deleted AR.

## Support for sending Approval Workflow error notifications to non-admin users

By default, notifications about errors in an Approval Workflow are only sent to users with the Client Admin role. Because users in this role aren't always the best person to diagnose and fix problems in Approval Workflows, we've added the option to send notifications about Approval Workflow errors to users in non-admin roles.

Now, you can turn on the new role permissions setting Approval Workflows > Receive Approval Workflow Error Notification for any role: when this setting is turned on for a role, users with the role receive notifications when errors occur in Approval Workflows. With this new feature, you can ensure Approval Workflow problems are resolved quickly by alerting the right people when they occur.

To learn more about these features and changes, download the [Hive9 User Guide](https://knowledge.uptempo.io/doc/BM_Uptempo_en_105000000000/Content/03%20Hive9/Hive9.htm).



# Release: October 2025

The October 2025 release for Hive9 is available from October 29, 2025, and adds these features to the platform:

## Support for monthly currency conversion rates on Purchase Requests

Previously, Purchase Requests in Hive9 could only use a single annual foreign exchange (FX) conversion rate for each currency. To enable more granular and time-sensitive conversions that better reflect real-world accounting practices, Hive9 now supports setting monthly FX rates for Purchase Requests.

Monthly FX rates were already supported for Actuals, and you can now apply the same rates to Purchase Requests: under Settings > Currency, there is now a new Use Monthly Rates for Purchase Requests setting for each currency. As with Actuals, enabling this option will apply the conversion rates set in the Monthly Exchange Rates fields to Purchase Requests in the specified currency (if this option is not enabled, the currency will use the set annual conversion rate, as before).

## Customize the default attribute to search on Hive9 grids

As an administrator, you can now choose which attribute Hive9's grid search will use by default. Until now, the search function on the Plan, Budget & Cost, and Calendar grids defaulted to searching by the Name attribute, and users had to manually change the attribute to search. If your organization typically uses an attribute other than Name to search for records in Hive9 (such as ID), you can now use the Default Search Attribute for Grids setting to set that attribute as the default for your users.

## Highlighted changes in Purchase Request update Excel files

When a Purchase Request (PR) is updated, the cells with changed values are now highlighted in the Excel file attached to the PR update email. This enhancement makes it easier for reviewers to identify what has changed compared to previous versions, helping to save time and reduce errors.

To learn more about these features and changes, download the [Hive9 User Guide](https://knowledge.uptempo.io/doc/BM_Uptempo_en_105000000000/Content/03%20Hive9/Hive9.htm).



# Release: September 2025

The September 2025 release for Hive9 is available from September 24, 2025, and adds these features to the platform:

## Added Amazon S3 bucket support to Measure Extract service

Measure Extract, Hive9's data ingestion service, now supports the Amazon S3 protocol. If you use AWS to store files, this means you can now ingest and process files into Hive9 directly from your Amazon S3 bucket.

This new capability can pull all files from a configured S3 bucket, and parse and process them in Hive9 in the same way as other sources. With the appropriate permissions, Measure Extract can also automatically files into an "Archive" folder in the same directory path on the S3 bucket after they have been successfully processed.

## Automatically collapse sections in Inspection Window

To enhance the performance of the Tactic Inspection Window, all sections (except for the main details section) are now automatically collapsed (closed) when the window is opened. This ensures that the Tactic Inspection window loads quickly, and only loads other sections as and when they are needed.

To learn more about these features and changes, download the [Hive9 User Guide](https://knowledge.uptempo.io/doc/BM_Uptempo_en_105000000000/Content/03%20Hive9/Hive9.htm).



# Release: August 2025

The August 2025 release for Hive9 is available from August 27, 2025, and adds these features to the platform:

## Support for importing custom dimensions and metrics from Google Analytics 4

Hive9’s Google Analytics 4 (GA4) data ingestion feature has been enhanced to support custom dimensions and metrics. This update gives you greater flexibility in aligning GA4 data imports with your unique reporting needs, enabling more tailored analysis and deeper insights within Hive9.

## Budget Configuration settings changes now tracked in Audit Log

All changes made on the Organization Setup > Budget Configuration settings page in Hive9 are now recorded in the Audit Log, allowing administrators to easily understand any changes made to financial workflows or budgeting logic. As with other tracked events, you can view all events related to Budget Configuration settings changes in the standard Hive9 Audit Log interface. Each event captures:

* Timestamp: The time and date the change was made

* User: Details of the user who made the change

* Value Details: The previous settings value before the change, and the new value after the change

* Field Name: The name of the field affected by the change

* Entity or Module: Specified as "Organization Settings - Budget Configuration" for all changes to the Budget Configuration settings page. Tracking these changes in the Audit Log provides transparency, ensures accountability and compliance, and helps with troubleshooting issues that might arise out of configuration changes.

## 

Section label changes on Organization Setup page

To more clearly communicate how to use the "Hive9 Hierarchy Naming" and "Custom Goals" settings on the Organization Setup page, we have updated the settings labels in these sections. The revised labels are designed to be more user-friendly and easier for new users to understand. The following labels have been changed:

Hive9 Hierarchy Naming section:

* Old Label: Plan → New Label: Level 1

* Old Label: Campaign → New Label: Level 2

* Old Label: Program → New Label: Level 3

* Old Label: Tactic → New Label: Level 4

* Old Label: Line Item → New Label: Level 5

Custom Goals section:

* Old Label: CustomGoalTypeCode → New Label: Goal Code

* Old Label: CustomGoalTitle → New Label: Goal Name

* Old Label: CustomGoalDataType → New Label: Goal Type

* Old Label: CustomGoalDescription → New Label: Description

To learn more about these features and changes and how to use them, download the [Hive9 User Guide](https://knowledge.uptempo.io/doc/BM_Uptempo_en_105000000000/Content/03%20Hive9/Hive9.htm).



# Release: July 2025

The July 2025 release for Hive9 is available from July 30, 2025, and adds these features to the platform:

## Custom notifications for non-date attribute

You can receive notifications when the status of your tactics, campaigns, or programs changes. Track approvals, ownership transfers, and workflow updates without manually checking dates. Select the attribute changes that matter to you and how you want to be alerted: in-app, email, or both.

## Web interface for uploading financial transactions to Hive9

You can now upload financial transactions directly in Hive9. Simply click the new Upload Transactions button, download our template for the correct format, and import your transaction data. This feature requires the `Upload Financial Transactions via UI` permission to be enabled for your role.

## Support for formula-based custom attributes at the PR Transactions level

You can now use formulas in custom attributes to automate calculations and dynamically populate fields within PR Transactions. When you create or edit a custom attribute for PR Transactions, you can now use formulas in the same way as you can in other hierarchy levels (for example Plan, Campaign, Program, Tactic, Line Items). Formula-based values in custom attribute fields are calculated whenever a PR Transaction is created or updated, and are displayed as read-only in the POR Transaction form and reports. For help with configuring formulas at the PR Transaction level, please reach out to your Customer Success Manager.

To learn more about these features and changes and how to use them, download the [Hive9 User Guide](https://knowledge.uptempo.io/doc/BM_Uptempo_en_105000000000/Content/03%20Hive9/Hive9.htm).



# Release: June 2025

The June 2025 release for Hive9 is available from June 25, 2025, and adds these features to the platform:

## Prevent Disruptions from Inactive Approval Workflows

You can now safely manage approval workflows without breaking active processes, as Hive9 blocks deactivation or deletion of workflows currently in progress and provides clear guidance when editing tactics with inactive workflows. The system automatically prevents approval actions on tactics using deactivated workflows, while still showing you which workflows are assigned. So you always understand what needs to be updated before you can move forward with your changes or approvals.

## Share Customized Plan and B&C Grid Views

With the latest update, users can now share their personally configured grid layouts and filters between Plan and B&C grids with other users. This collaborative feature eliminates redundant configuration work across teams, as recipients can access your shared views directly from their My Saved Views section in Plan grid, complete with all column selections, display order preferences, and filter settings intact.

## Terminology Changes in UI

With shared grid views, we’ve updated terminology throughout the views functionality to make the interface more intuitive and consistent. The term Views replaces Filter views to reflect what users actually interact with. Button labels now clearly match their actions, particularly changing the confirmation button in the sharing dialog from Save to Share to better represent its function.

| Old Terminology |New Label |Location |
----------------------------------------
| My Saved Filter Views |My Saved Views |Section header |
| Save New Filter Set |Save New View |Button in My Saved Views section |
| Save this Filter View |Save this View |Tooltip on Save button |
| - |Share Filter: view name |Title in Share popup |
| Save |Share |Button at bottom of Share popup |

To learn more about these features and changes and how to use them, download the [Hive9 User Guide](https://knowledge.uptempo.io/doc/BM_Uptempo_en_105000000000/Content/03%20Hive9/Hive9.htm).



# Release: May 2025

The May 2025 release for Hive9 is available from May 28, 2025, and adds these features to the platform:

## Finance Budget Link for Multi-Year Tactics Mandatory

You must now link finance budgets to all line-items in each year of your multi-year tactics. When creating or editing line-items in any year in a single tactic spanning multiple years, ensure every fiscal year has a budget link. Otherwise, you will see a validation message. This message will require you to add the missing links before saving. This applies to all Hive9 dialogs and windows where you manage line items.

Note: Note

This validation is applicable only if the corresponding setting is turned ON at the “Org. Setup” screen.

## Save Column Order in Grids

You will no longer lose your carefully arranged column layouts when making grid modifications. Previously, adding or removing columns would reset your entire column arrangement. With this version, your previously set personal column sequence is retained for all changes to plan, B&C, PR and AR grids.

## Reset Selected Pre-Saved Filters in Grids

The Set Default View feature lets you reset your default view with a single click. Your default view can be a Saved Preset or another filtered/unfiltered view which you have not saved as a preset. You find “Set Default View” in the My Saved Views dropdown in Plan, B&C, PR, and AR grids. This reset applies only to your current session. To make this state persist across sessions, you must make sure the desired Preset has been marked as Default.

## Asana Integration

This connector was released in March 2025. It is only available to a select group of customers. It allows you syncing tactics as nested tasks under parent projects in Asana while providing customizable configurations for pushing tasks based on tactic type. Template sync options have been relocated to the Integration Instance screen with improved logging for better visibility.

## Terminology Changes in UI

We have updated the terminology across grids to make the interface more intuitive and consistent. The term Views replaces Filter Views to reflect what you actually interact with. Button labels now clearly match their actions.

| Old Terminology |New Label |Location |
----------------------------------------
| My Saved Filter Views |My Saved Views |Section header |
| Save New Filter Set |Save New View |Button in My Saved Views section |
| Save this Filter View |Save this View |Tooltip on Save button |

To learn more about these features and changes, download the [Hive9 User Guide](https://knowledge.uptempo.io/doc/BM_Uptempo_en_105000000000/Content/03%20Hive9/Hive9.htm).



# Release: February 2025

The February 2025 release for Hive9 is available from February 26, 2025, and adds these features to the platform:

## Administrators can log in as other users

With the new Log-in-As feature, administrators can now securely log in to Hive9 as any other user. This capability lets the administrator see the Hive9 platform exactly as that user does, making it easier to investigate and troubleshoot user issues, test permission configurations, and more. To ensure user privacy, this feature requires explicit user consent to be used, and offers configurable access duration controls.
![Hive9Log-In-As_thumb_450_0.png](images/Hive9Log-In-As.png)

## Customizable user inactivity timeouts

Administrators can now set a custom idle timeout duration for their Hive9 instance to help prevent unauthorized access and safeguard sensitive data. With the new Application Timeout setting, you can define how long (in minutes) an authenticated user's session will remain valid while they are inactive: if this time period elapses before the user has become active again, the user is automatically logged out of the system and must authenticate to regain access.

## 

New User Category attribute for user accounts

We have introduced a new mandatory attribute for user accounts that allows administrators to define user categories. When enabled, Hive9 users can assign themselves to a category on their profile using the new User Category attribute (the value can also be changed by authorized users with the appropriate permissions from the Org. Setup screen). All Hive9 entities (Plan, Campaign, Program, Tactic, Line Item) automatically inherit the entity owner's User Category attribute value, allowing this attribute to be used to trigger dependencies on other Custom Attributes specific to each value.

## Prevent deletion of Tactics connected to BGETs

If Budget General Expense Transactions (BGETs) integration is enabled in a Hive9 instance, the system now helps to ensure data integrity by preventing deletion of records associated with BGETs. If a user attempts to delete a Tactic, Campaign, or Program that is linked to a BGET, they will now see an error message that they must manually remove the BGET ID before they can delete the selected entity.

To learn more about these features and changes, download the [Hive9 User Guide](https://knowledge.uptempo.io/doc/BM_Uptempo_en_105000000000/Content/03%20Hive9/Hive9.htm).



# Release: January 2025

The January 2025 release adds these features to the Hive9 platform:

## Allow Users to Select Only Line Items on Plan Grid

Users can now exclusively select line items on the Plan Grid and hide system-generated balance entries through a new Hierarchy Viewer option, while the Budget & Cost Grid requires parent object selection for optimal performance.
![H9select only plan grid](images/H9SelectOnlyPlanGrid.png)

## Export Transactions View from Tactic Inspection Window

Users can now export transaction details directly from any Tactic's Inspection Window into Excel, with data and columns matching the Transaction grid view shown in the Tactic Inspection Window and following standardized file name conventions.
![H9export transactions](images/H9ExportTransactions.png)

## Reassign Objects to Other User on User Deletion

When deleting a user, authorized users can now choose how to handle the Ownership of the Hive9 objects owned by the user being deleted. They can either keep the ownership to the same user to reassign all their owned objects to another active user. This feature helps in maintaining complete data continuity while sending notifications to new owners and tracking all ownership changes in an audit trail.

## Convert Transactions Screen to Kendo Grid

The Transactions grid has been augmented with Kendo UI components to facilitate seamless interactions for bulk operations and smoother handling of large data sets while maintaining familiar functionality.

## Bulk Linking Transactions with Single Line Item

A user-centric bulk transaction mapping feature introduces an intelligent Map Bulk Transactions button that activates upon multiple checkbox selections in the transaction grid. Through an innovative dual-section popup interface displaying synchronized transaction and line item grids alongside the Plan Picker, users can efficiently distribute line item amounts across multiple transactions while sophisticated validation ensures data integrity by preventing duplicate mappings and invalid amount allocations.

To learn more about the new features, read the full [Hive9 User Guide](https://knowledge.uptempo.io/doc/BM_Uptempo_en_108000000000/108000000000.pdf).



# Uptempo's feature release lifecycle

## Introduction

The feature release lifecycle is the process that Uptempo uses for adding new features to the Campaign Management and Financial Management products, and for removing features when they reach the end of their lifespan. It covers:

* How we make new features available to Uptempo customers

* How we communicate changes in a feature's status during each stage of the lifecycle.

The Uptempo product lifecycle consists of three release stages for new features, and two deprecation stages for retiring features.

This page describes these stages, and what you can expect at each stage.

## Release stages

We release new features to the Uptempo platform in the following stages:
